```text
1:\t"use client";
2:\t
3:\timport React, { useState } from "react";
4:\timport Modal from "@/components/modal/modal";
5:\timport adsService from "@/lib/api/ads.service";
6:\timport { AdStatus, AdActionType, ExecuteActionInput } from "@/types/ads.types";
7:\timport styles from "./ActionModal.module.css";
8:\t
9:\tinterface ActionModalProps {
10:\t  isOpen: boolean;
11:\t  onClose: () => void;
12:\t  adId: string;
13:\t  currentStatus: AdStatus;
14:\t  onSuccess: () => void;
15:\t}
16:\t
17:\tfunction ActionModal({
18:\t  isOpen,
19:\t  onClose,
20:\t  adId,
21:\t  currentStatus,
22:\t  onSuccess,
23:\t}: ActionModalProps) {
24:\t  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
25:\t  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
26:\t
27:\t  const [selectedAction, setSelectedAction] = useState<AdActionType | "">("");
28:\t  const [rejectionReason, setRejectionReason] = useState("");
29:\t  const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState("");
30:\t  const [endDate, setEndDate] = useState("");
31:\t  const [reason, setReason] = useState("");
32:\t
33:\t  const [errors, setErrors] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});
34:\t  const [touched, setTouched] = useState<Record<string, boolean>>({});
35:\t
36:\t  const availableActions = adsService.getAvailableActions(currentStatus);
37:\t
38:\t  const actionDefinitions: Record<string, { label: string; description: string; variant: string }> = {
39:\t    approve: {
40:\t      label: "Approve",
41:\t      description: "Move ad to PENDING_PAYMENT status",
42:\t      variant: "success",
43:\t    },
44:\t    reject: {
45:\t      label: "Reject",
46:\t      description: "Move ad to REJECTED status",
47:\t      variant: "danger",
48:\t    },
49:\t    schedule: {
50:\t      label: "Schedule",
51:\t      description: "Set start date and move to SCHEDULED",
52:\t      variant: "primary",
53:\t    },
54:\t    pause: {
55:\t      label: "Pause",
56:\t      description: "Temporarily pause running ad",
57:\t      variant: "warning",
58:\t    },
59:\t    resume: {
60:\t      label: "Resume",
61:\t      description: "Resume paused ad",
62:\t      variant: "success",
63:\t    },
64:\t    cancel: {
65:\t      label: "Cancel",
66:\t      description: "Cancel ad request",
67:\t      variant: "danger",
68:\t    },
69:\t    complete: {
70:\t      label: "Complete",
71:\t      description: "Mark ad as completed",
72:\t      variant: "success",
73:\t    },
74:\t  };
75:\t
76:\t  const validateField = (field: string, value: string) => {
77:\t    const newErrors = { ...errors };
78:\t
79:\t    switch (field) {
80:\t      case "rejectionReason":
81:\t        if (selectedAction === "reject") {
82:\t          if (!value) {
83:\t            newErrors.rejectionReason = "Rejection reason is required for reject action";
84:\t          } else if (value.length < 10) {
85:\t            newErrors.rejectionReason = "Rejection reason must be at least 10 characters";
86:\t          } else {
87:\t            delete newErrors.rejectionReason;
88:\t          }
89:\t        } else {
90:\t          delete newErrors.rejectionReason;
91:\t        }
92:\t        break;
93:\t      case "startDate":
94:\t        if (selectedAction === "schedule") {
95:\t          if (!value) {
96:\t            newErrors.startDate = "Start date is required for schedule action";
97:\t          } else {
98:\t            const date = new Date(value);
99:\t            const today = new Date();
100:\t            today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0);
101:\t            if (date < today) {
102:\t              newErrors.startDate = "Start date cannot be in the past";
103:\t            } else {
104:\t              delete newErrors.startDate;
105:\t            }
106:\t          }
107:\t        } else {
108:\t          delete newErrors.startDate;
109:\t        }
110:\t        break;
111:\t      case "endDate":
112:\t        if (selectedAction === "schedule" && value && startDate) {
113:\t          const start = new Date(startDate);
114:\t          const end = new Date(value);
115:\t          if (end <= start) {
116:\t            newErrors.endDate = "End date must be after start date";
117:\t          } else {
118:\t            delete newErrors.endDate;
119:\t          }
120:\t        } else {
121:\t          delete newErrors.endDate;
122:\t        }
123:\t        break;
124:\t    }
125:\t
126:\t    setErrors(newErrors);
127:\t  };
128:\t
129:\t  const handleBlur = (field: string) => {
130:\t    setTouched({ ...touched, [field]: true });
131:\t  };
132:\t
133:\t  const handleActionChange = (action: AdActionType) => {
134:\t    setSelectedAction(action);
135:\t    setErrors({});
136:\t    setTouched({});
137:\t  };
138:\t
139:\t  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
140:\t    e.preventDefault();
141:\t
142:\t    if (!selectedAction) {
143:\t      setError("Please select an action");
144:\t      return;
145:\t    }
146:\t
147:\t    validateField("rejectionReason", rejectionReason);
148:\t    validateField("startDate", startDate);
149:\t    validateField("endDate", endDate);
150:\t
151:\t    setTouched({
152:\t      rejectionReason: true,
153:\t      startDate: true,
154:\t      endDate: true,
155:\t      reason: true,
156:\t    });
157:\t
158:\t    if (Object.keys(errors).length > 0) {
159:\t      return;
160:\t    }
161:\t
162:\t    try {
163:\t      setLoading(true);
164:\t      setError(null);
165:\t
166:\t      const data: ExecuteActionInput = {
167:\t        action: selectedAction,
168:\t      };
169:\t
170:\t      if (selectedAction === "reject" && rejectionReason) {
171:\t        data.rejection_reason = rejectionReason;
172:\t      }
173:\t      if (selectedAction === "schedule") {
174:\t        if (startDate) data.start_date = startDate;
175:\t        if (endDate) data.end_date = endDate;
176:\t      }
177:\t      if (selectedAction === "cancel" && reason) {
178:\t        data.reason = reason;
179:\t      }
180:\t
181:\t      const response = await adsService.executeAction(adId, data);
182:\t
183:\t      if (response.success) {
184:\t        onSuccess();
185:\t        onClose();
186:\t      } else {
187:\t        setError("Failed to execute action");
188:\t      }
189:\t    } catch (err: unknown) {
190:\t      const errorMessage = err instanceof Error && 'response' in err 
191:\t        ? (err as { response?: { data?: { message?: string } } }).response?.data?.message 
192:\t        : "Failed to execute action";
193:\t      setError(errorMessage || "Failed to execute action");
194:\t    } finally {
195:\t      setLoading(false);
196:\t    }
197:\t  };
198:\t
199:\t  return (
200:\t    <Modal title="Execute Action" isOpen={isOpen} onClose={onClose} size="md">
201:\t      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className={styles.form}>
202:\t        {error && <div className={styles.error}>{error}</div>}
203:\t
204:\t        <div className={styles.actionsGrid}>
205:\t          {availableActions.map((action) => {
206:\t            const def = actionDefinitions[action];
207:\t            const isSelected = selectedAction === action;
208:\t            return (
209:\t              <label
210:\t                key={action}
211:\t                className={`${styles.actionCard} ${isSelected ? styles.selected : ""} ${styles[def.variant] || ""}`}
212:\t              >
213:\t                <input
214:\t                  type="radio"
215:\t                  name="action"
216:\t                  value={action}
217:\t                  checked={isSelected}
218:\t                  onChange={() => handleActionChange(action as AdActionType)}
219:\t                />
220:\t                <div className={styles.actionCardInner}>
221:\t                  <div className={styles.actionCardContent}>
222:\t                    <div className={styles.radioCircle}>
223:\t                      <div className={styles.radioDot}></div>
224:\t                    </div>
225:\t                    <div className={styles.actionInfo}>
226:\t                      <h4>{def.label}</h4>
227:\t                      <p>{def.description}</p>
228:\t                    </div>
229:\t                  </div>
230:\t                </div>
231:\t              </label>
232:\t            );
233:\t          })}
234:\t        </div>
235:\t
236:\t        {selectedAction === "reject" && (
237:\t          <div className={styles.fieldGroup}>
238:\t            <label className={styles.label}>
239:\t              Rejection Reason <span className={styles.required}>*</span>
240:\t            </label>
241:\t            <textarea
242:\t              className={styles.textarea}
243:\t              value={rejectionReason}
244:\t              onChange={(e) => {

246:\t                validateField("rejectionReason", e.target.value);
247:\t              }}
248:\t              onBlur={() => handleBlur("rejectionReason")}
249:\t              placeholder="Explain why this ad is being rejected..."
250:\t            />
251:\t            {touched.rejectionReason && errors.rejectionReason && (
```

```text
1:\t"""
2:\tAdmin Rental Service
3:\t============================================================
4:\t
5:\tThis module contains service classes for admin rental issue management operations.
6:\t
7:\tCreated: 2025-11-06
8:\t"""
9:\t
10:\tfrom typing import Dict, Any
11:\tfrom django.db import transaction
12:\tfrom django.utils import timezone
13:\tfrom django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
14:\t
15:\tfrom api.common.services.base import BaseService, ServiceException
16:\tfrom api.user.rentals.models import Rental, RentalIssue
17:\t
18:\tUser = get_user_model()
19:\t
20:\t
21:\tclass AdminRentalService(BaseService):
22:\t    """Service for admin rental issue management"""
23:\t    
24:\t    def get_rental_issues(
25:\t        self,
26:\t        status: str = None,
27:\t        issue_type: str = None,
28:\t        search: str = None,
29:\t        ordering: str = '-reported_at'
30:\t    ):
31:\t        """
32:\t        Get list of rental issues with filtering
33:\t        
34:\t        Args:
35:\t            status: Filter by status (REPORTED, RESOLVED)
36:\t            issue_type: Filter by issue type
37:\t            search: Search in description or rental code
38:\t            ordering: Order by field
39:\t            
40:\t        Returns:
41:\t            Queryset of rental issues
42:\t        """
43:\t        queryset = RentalIssue.objects.select_related(
44:\t            'rental__user',
45:\t            'rental__power_bank',
46:\t            'rental__station',
47:\t            'rental__return_station'
48:\t        )
49:\t        
50:\t        # Apply filters
51:\t        if status:
52:\t            queryset = queryset.filter(status=status)
53:\t        
54:\t        if issue_type:
55:\t            queryset = queryset.filter(issue_type=issue_type)
56:\t        
57:\t        if search:
58:\t            from django.db.models import Q
59:\t            queryset = queryset.filter(
60:\t                Q(description__icontains=search) |
61:\t                Q(rental__rental_code__icontains=search)
62:\t            )
63:\t        
64:\t        # Apply ordering
65:\t        queryset = queryset.order_by(ordering)
66:\t        
67:\t        return queryset
68:\t    
69:\t    def get_rental_issue(self, issue_id: str) -> RentalIssue:
70:\t        """
71:\t        Get specific rental issue by ID
72:\t        
73:\t        Args:
74:\t            issue_id: Issue ID
75:\t            
76:\t        Returns:
77:\t            RentalIssue instance
78:\t            
79:\t        Raises:
80:\t            ServiceException: If issue not found
81:\t        """
82:\t        try:
83:\t            return RentalIssue.objects.select_related(
84:\t                'rental__user',
85:\t                'rental__power_bank',
86:\t                'rental__station',
87:\t                'rental__return_station'
88:\t            ).get(id=issue_id)
89:\t        except RentalIssue.DoesNotExist:
90:\t            raise ServiceException(
91:\t                detail="Rental issue not found",
92:\t                code="rental_issue_not_found"
93:\t            )
94:\t    
95:\t    @transaction.atomic
96:\t    def update_rental_issue_status(
97:\t        self,
98:\t        issue_id: str,
99:\t        status: str,
100:\t        admin_user: User,
101:\t        notes: str = None,
102:\t        request=None
103:\t    ) -> RentalIssue:
104:\t        """
105:\t        Update rental issue status
106:\t        
107:\t        Args:
108:\t            issue_id: Issue ID
109:\t            status: New status (REPORTED, RESOLVED)
110:\t            admin_user: Admin user making the update
111:\t            notes: Optional admin notes
112:\t            request: HTTP request for audit logging
113:\t            
114:\t        Returns:
115:\t            Updated RentalIssue instance
116:\t            
117:\t        Raises:
118:\t            ServiceException: If validation fails
119:\t        """
120:\t        issue = self.get_rental_issue(issue_id)
121:\t        
122:\t        # Validate status
123:\t        valid_statuses = ['REPORTED', 'RESOLVED']
124:\t        if status not in valid_statuses:
125:\t            raise ServiceException(
126:\t                detail=f"Invalid status. Must be one of: {', '.join(valid_statuses)}",
127:\t                code="invalid_status"
128:\t            )
129:\t        
130:\t        # Update status
131:\t        old_status = issue.status
132:\t        issue.status = status
133:\t        
134:\t        # Set resolved_at if resolved
135:\t        if status == 'RESOLVED' and old_status != 'RESOLVED':
136:\t            issue.resolved_at = timezone.now()
137:\t        
138:\t        issue.save(update_fields=['status', 'resolved_at', 'updated_at'])
139:\t        
140:\t        # Log admin action
141:\t        self._log_admin_action(
142:\t            admin_user=admin_user,
143:\t            action_type='UPDATE_RENTAL_ISSUE_STATUS',
144:\t            target_model='RentalIssue',
145:\t            target_id=str(issue.id),
146:\t            changes={
147:\t                'old_status': old_status,
148:\t                'new_status': status,
149:\t                'rental_code': issue.rental.rental_code,
150:\t                'notes': notes
151:\t            },
152:\t            description=f"Updated rental issue {issue.id} status from {old_status} to {status}",
153:\t            request=request
154:\t        )
155:\t        
156:\t        self.log_info(f"Rental issue {issue.id} status updated: {old_status} -> {status}")
157:\t        
158:\t        # Send notification to user if resolved
159:\t        if status == 'RESOLVED':
160:\t            self._send_issue_resolved_notification(issue)
161:\t        
162:\t        return issue
163:\t    
164:\t    @transaction.atomic
165:\t    def delete_rental_issue(
166:\t        self,
167:\t        issue_id: str,
168:\t        admin_user: User,
169:\t        request=None
170:\t    ) -> Dict[str, str]:
171:\t        """
172:\t        Delete rental issue (soft delete by marking as resolved)
173:\t        
174:\t        Args:
175:\t            issue_id: Issue ID
176:\t            admin_user: Admin user making the deletion
177:\t            request: HTTP request for audit logging
178:\t            
179:\t        Returns:
180:\t            Success message dict
181:\t            
182:\t        Raises:
183:\t            ServiceException: If validation fails
184:\t        """
185:\t        issue = self.get_rental_issue(issue_id)
186:\t        
187:\t        # Soft delete by marking as resolved
188:\t        if issue.status != 'RESOLVED':
189:\t            issue.status = 'RESOLVED'
190:\t            issue.resolved_at = timezone.now()
191:\t            issue.save(update_fields=['status', 'resolved_at', 'updated_at'])
192:\t        
193:\t        # Log admin action
194:\t        self._log_admin_action(
195:\t            admin_user=admin_user,
196:\t            action_type='DELETE_RENTAL_ISSUE',
197:\t            target_model='RentalIssue',
198:\t            target_id=str(issue.id),
199:\t            changes={
200:\t                'rental_code': issue.rental.rental_code,
201:\t                'issue_type': issue.issue_type
202:\t            },
203:\t            description=f"Deleted rental issue {issue.id}",
204:\t            request=request
205:\t        )
206:\t        
207:\t        self.log_info(f"Rental issue {issue.id} deleted")
208:\t        
209:\t        return {"message": "Rental issue deleted successfully"}
210:\t    
211:\t    def _send_issue_resolved_notification(self, issue: RentalIssue):
212:\t        """Send notification to user when issue is resolved"""
213:\t        try:
214:\t            from api.user.notifications.services import notify
215:\t            
216:\t            notify(
217:\t                issue.rental.user,
218:\t                'rental_issue_resolved',
219:\t                rental_code=issue.rental.rental_code,
220:\t                issue_type=issue.issue_type,
221:\t                rental_id=str(issue.rental.id),
222:\t                issue_id=str(issue.id)
223:\t            )
224:\t        except Exception as e:
225:\t            self.log_warning(f"Failed to send issue resolved notification: {str(e)}")
226:\t    
227:\t    def _log_admin_action(
228:\t        self,
229:\t        admin_user: User,
230:\t        action_type: str,
231:\t        target_model: str,
232:\t        target_id: str,
233:\t        changes: dict,
234:\t        description: str,
235:\t        request=None
236:\t    ):
237:\t        """Log admin action to audit trail"""
238:\t        try:
239:\t            from api.admin.models import AdminActionLog
240:\t            
241:\t            AdminActionLog.objects.create(
242:\t                admin_user=admin_user,
243:\t                action_type=action_type,
244:\t                target_model=target_model,
245:\t                target_id=target_id,
246:\t                changes=changes,
247:\t                description=description,
248:\t                ip_address=self._get_client_ip(request) if request else '',
249:\t                user_agent=request.META.get('HTTP_USER_AGENT', '') if request else ''
250:\t            )
251:\t        except Exception as e:
252:\t            self.log_error(f"Failed to log admin action: {str(e)}")
253:\t    
254:\t    def _get_client_ip(self, request) -> str:
255:\t        """Get client IP address from request"""
256:\t        x_forwarded_for = request.META.get('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR')
257:\t        if x_forwarded_for:
258:\t            ip = x_forwarded_for.split(',')[0]
259:\t        else:
260:\t            ip = request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR')
261:\t        return ip
262:\t    
263:\t    # ============================================================
264:\t    # Rental List Methods
265:\t    # ============================================================
266:\t    
267:\t    def get_rentals_list(self, filters: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
268:\t        """
269:\t        Get paginated list of rentals with filters
270:\t        
271:\t        Args:
272:\t            filters: Dictionary containing filter parameters
273:\t                - status: Filter by rental status
274:\t                - payment_status: Filter by payment status
275:\t                - user_id: Filter by user ID
276:\t                - station_id: Filter by station ID
277:\t                - search: Search by rental code or user name
278:\t                - recent: 'today', '24h', '7d', '30d' for recent rentals
279:\t                - start_date: Filter rentals started after this date
280:\t                - end_date: Filter rentals started before this date
281:\t                - page: Page number (default: 1)
282:\t                - page_size: Items per page (default: 20)
283:\t                
284:\t        Returns:
285:\t            Dict with results and pagination info
286:\t        """
287:\t        from api.common.utils.helpers import paginate_queryset
288:\t        from datetime import timedelta
289:\t        from django.db.models import Q
290:\t        
291:\t        queryset = self._get_base_rental_queryset()
292:\t        
293:\t        if filters:
294:\t            # Status filter
295:\t            if filters.get('status'):
296:\t                queryset = queryset.filter(status=filters['status'])
297:\t            
298:\t            # Payment status filter
299:\t            if filters.get('payment_status'):
300:\t                queryset = queryset.filter(payment_status=filters['payment_status'])
301:\t            
302:\t            # User filter
303:\t            if filters.get('user_id'):
304:\t                queryset = queryset.filter(user_id=filters['user_id'])
305:\t            
306:\t            # Station filter
307:\t            if filters.get('station_id'):
308:\t                queryset = queryset.filter(
309:\t                    Q(station_id=filters['station_id']) | 
310:\t                    Q(return_station_id=filters['station_id'])
311:\t                )
312:\t            
313:\t            # Search filter
314:\t            if filters.get('search'):
315:\t                search_term = filters['search']
316:\t                queryset = queryset.filter(
317:\t                    Q(rental_code__icontains=search_term) |
318:\t                    Q(user__username__icontains=search_term) |
319:\t                    Q(user__phone_number__icontains=search_term)
320:\t                )
321:\t            
322:\t            # Recent rentals filter
323:\t            if filters.get('recent'):
324:\t                now = timezone.now()
325:\t                recent_type = filters['recent']
326:\t                
327:\t                if recent_type == 'today':
328:\t                    queryset = queryset.filter(
329:\t                        created_at__date=now.date()
330:\t                    )
331:\t                elif recent_type == '24h':
332:\t                    queryset = queryset.filter(
333:\t                        created_at__gte=now - timedelta(hours=24)
334:\t                    )
335:\t                elif recent_type == '7d':
336:\t                    queryset = queryset.filter(
337:\t                        created_at__gte=now - timedelta(days=7)
338:\t                    )
339:\t                elif recent_type == '30d':
340:\t                    queryset = queryset.filter(
341:\t                        created_at__gte=now - timedelta(days=30)
342:\t                    )
343:\t            
344:\t            # Date range filters
345:\t            if filters.get('start_date'):
346:\t                queryset = queryset.filter(started_at__gte=filters['start_date'])
347:\t            
348:\t            if filters.get('end_date'):
349:\t                queryset = queryset.filter(started_at__lte=filters['end_date'])
350:\t        
351:\t        # Order by latest first
352:\t        queryset = queryset.order_by('-created_at')
353:\t        
354:\t        # Pagination
355:\t        page = filters.get('page', 1) if filters else 1
356:\t        page_size = filters.get('page_size', 20) if filters else 20
357:\t        
358:\t        return paginate_queryset(queryset, page, page_size)
359:\t    
360:\t    def get_rental_detail(self, rental_id: str):
361:\t        """
362:\t        Get detailed rental information
363:\t        
364:\t        Args:
365:\t            rental_id: Rental ID (UUID)
366:\t            
367:\t        Returns:
368:\t            Rental instance with all related data
369:\t            
370:\t        Raises:
371:\t            ServiceException: If rental not found
372:\t        """
373:\t        from api.user.rentals.models import Rental
374:\t        
375:\t        try:
376:\t            return self._get_base_rental_queryset().get(id=rental_id)
377:\t        except Rental.DoesNotExist:
378:\t            raise ServiceException(
379:\t                detail="Rental not found",
380:\t                code="rental_not_found"
381:\t            )
382:\t
383:\t    @transaction.atomic
384:\t    def update_rental_manual_completion(
385:\t        self,
386:\t        rental_id: str,
387:\t        amount,
388:\t        status: str,
389:\t        admin_user: User,
390:\t        request=None
391:\t    ) -> Rental:
392:\t        """
393:\t        Simple admin override to adjust amount and status.
394:\t        Keeps payment as due when rental is completed manually.
395:\t        """
396:\t        rental = Rental.objects.select_for_update().filter(id=rental_id).first()
397:\t        if not rental:
398:\t            raise ServiceException(detail="Rental not found", code="rental_not_found")
399:\t
400:\t        normalized_status = str(status or '').strip().upper()
401:\t        valid_statuses = [choice[0] for choice in Rental.RENTAL_STATUS_CHOICES]
402:\t        if normalized_status not in valid_statuses:
403:\t            raise ServiceException(
404:\t                detail=f"Invalid rental status. Must be one of: {', '.join(valid_statuses)}",
405:\t                code="invalid_rental_status"
406:\t            )
407:\t
408:\t        old_status = rental.status
409:\t        old_payment_status = rental.payment_status
410:\t
411:\t        rental.status = normalized_status
412:\t        rental.overdue_amount = amount
413:\t        update_fields = ['status', 'overdue_amount', 'updated_at']
414:\t
415:\t        if normalized_status == 'COMPLETED':
416:\t            if not rental.ended_at:
417:\t                rental.ended_at = timezone.now()
418:\t                update_fields.append('ended_at')
419:\t            rental.is_returned_on_time = bool(rental.due_at and rental.ended_at and rental.ended_at <= rental.due_at)
420:\t            rental.payment_status = 'PENDING'
421:\t            update_fields.extend(['is_returned_on_time', 'payment_status'])
422:\t
423:\t            rental.rental_metadata = dict(rental.rental_metadata or {})
424:\t            rental.rental_metadata['manual_return_override'] = True
425:\t            rental.rental_metadata['manual_return_override_at'] = timezone.now().isoformat()
426:\t            rental.rental_metadata['manual_return_override_by_admin'] = str(admin_user.id)
427:\t            update_fields.append('rental_metadata')
428:\t
429:\t        rental.save(update_fields=list(dict.fromkeys(update_fields)))
430:\t
431:\t        self._log_admin_action(
432:\t            admin_user=admin_user,
433:\t            action_type='UPDATE_RENTAL_MANUAL_COMPLETION',
434:\t            target_model='Rental',
435:\t            target_id=str(rental.id),
436:\t            changes={
437:\t                'rental_code': rental.rental_code,
438:\t                'old_status': old_status,
439:\t                'new_status': rental.status,
440:\t                'old_payment_status': old_payment_status,
441:\t                'new_payment_status': rental.payment_status,
442:\t                'amount': str(rental.overdue_amount),
443:\t            },
444:\t            description=f"Manual rental update: {rental.rental_code} -> {rental.status}",
445:\t            request=request
446:\t        )
447:\t
448:\t        return rental
449:\t    
450:\t    def _get_base_rental_queryset(self):
451:\t        """Get base queryset with all necessary relationships"""
452:\t        from api.user.rentals.models import Rental
453:\t        
454:\t        return Rental.objects.select_related(
455:\t            'user',
456:\t            'station',
457:\t            'return_station',
458:\t            'slot',
459:\t            'package',
460:\t            'power_bank'
461:\t        ).prefetch_related(
462:\t            'extensions',
463:\t            'issues'
464:\t        )

```

```text
                 detail="Rental not found",
                 code="rental_not_found"
             )
+
+    @transaction.atomic
+    def update_rental_manual_completion(
+        self,
+        rental_id: str,
+        status: str,
+        admin_user: User,
+        amount=None,
+        request=None
+    ) -> Rental:
+        """
+        Simple admin override to adjust amount and status.
+        Keeps payment as due when rental is completed manually.
+        """
+        rental = Rental.objects.select_for_update().filter(id=rental_id).first()
+        if not rental:
+            raise ServiceException(detail="Rental not found", code="rental_not_found")
+
+        normalized_status = str(status or '').strip().upper()
+        valid_statuses = [choice[0] for choice in Rental.RENTAL_STATUS_CHOICES]
+        if normalized_status not in valid_statuses:
+            raise ServiceException(
+                detail=f"Invalid rental status. Must be one of: {', '.join(valid_statuses)}",
+                code="invalid_rental_status"
+            )
+
+        old_status = rental.status
+        old_payment_status = rental.payment_status
+        old_overdue_amount = rental.overdue_amount
+
+        rental.status = normalized_status
+        update_fields = ['status', 'updated_at']
+        if amount is not None:
+            rental.overdue_amount = amount
+            update_fields.append('overdue_amount')
+
+        if normalized_status == 'COMPLETED':
+            if not rental.ended_at:
+                rental.ended_at = timezone.now()
+                update_fields.append('ended_at')
+            rental.is_returned_on_time = bool(rental.due_at and rental.ended_at and rental.ended_at <= rental.due_at)
+            rental.payment_status = 'PENDING'
+            update_fields.extend(['is_returned_on_time', 'payment_status'])
+
+            rental.rental_metadata = dict(rental.rental_metadata or {})
+            rental.rental_metadata['manual_return_override'] = True
+            rental.rental_metadata['manual_return_override_at'] = timezone.now().isoformat()
+            rental.rental_metadata['manual_return_override_by_admin'] = str(admin_user.id)
+            update_fields.append('rental_metadata')
+
+        rental.save(update_fields=list(dict.fromkeys(update_fields)))
+
+        self._log_admin_action(
+            admin_user=admin_user,
+            action_type='UPDATE_RENTAL_MANUAL_COMPLETION',
+            target_model='Rental',
+            target_id=str(rental.id),
+            changes={
+                'rental_code': rental.rental_code,
+                'old_status': old_status,
+                'new_status': rental.status,
+                'old_payment_status': old_payment_status,
+                'new_payment_status': rental.payment_status,
+                'old_amount': str(old_overdue_amount),
+                'new_amount': str(rental.overdue_amount),
+            },
+            description=f"Manual rental update: {rental.rental_code} -> {rental.status}",
+            request=request
+        )
+
+        return rental
     
     def _get_base_rental_queryset(self):
         """Get base queryset with all necessary relationships"""

+@rental_router.register(r"admin/rentals/<str:rental_id>/force-complete", name="admin-rental-force-complete")
+@extend_schema(
+    tags=["Admin - Rentals"],
+    summary="Manual Rental Update",
+    description="Update rental status and amount manually (Staff only)",
+    request=AdminRentalManualUpdateSerializer,
+    responses={200: BaseResponseSerializer}
+)
+class AdminRentalForceCompleteView(GenericAPIView, BaseAPIView):
+    permission_classes = [IsStaffPermission]
+    serializer_class = AdminRentalManualUpdateSerializer
+
+    @log_api_call(include_request_data=True)
+    def post(self, request: Request, rental_id: str) -> Response:
+        """Manually update rental status and amount"""
+        def operation():
+            serializer = self.get_serializer(data=request.data)
+            serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)
+
+            service = AdminRentalService()
+            rental = service.update_rental_manual_completion(
+                rental_id=rental_id,
+                amount=serializer.validated_data.get('amount'),
+                status=serializer.validated_data['status'],
+                admin_user=request.user,
+                request=request
+            )
+
+            return serializers.AdminRentalDetailSerializer(rental).data
+
+        return self.handle_service_operation(
+            operation,
+            "Rental updated successfully",
+            "Failed to update rental"
+        )
+

+
```

```text
+    if (!normalizedStatus) {
+      toast.error("Status is required");

+

+    let parsedAmount: number | null = null;

+        toast.error("Please enter a valid non-negative amount");
+        return;
+      }

+          },

+        throw new Error(errorMessage);
+      }
+
+      toast.success("Rental updated successfully");
+      setIsForceCompleteModalOpen(false);
+      await fetchRentalDetail();

+      const message =
+        err?.message || "Failed to force complete rental";

+    }
```

```json
{"command":"$seed = @'\nfrom datetime import timedelta\nfrom decimal import Decimal\nimport json\nfrom django.utils import timezone\nfrom django.contrib.auth import get_user_model\nfrom api.user.stations.models import Station, StationSlot, PowerBank\nfrom api.user.rentals.models import Rental, RentalPackage\n\nUser = get_user_model()\nuser = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True).order_by('id').first() or User.objects.order_by('id').first()\npkg = RentalPackage.objects.filter(is_active=True).order_by('id').first()\nnow = timezone.now()\nsuffix = now.strftime('%m%d%H%M%S')\n\nstation = Station.objects.create(\n    station_name=f'Optional Amount Test {suffix}',\n    serial_number=f'OPT-SN-{suffix}',\n    imei=f'OPT-IMEI-{suffix}',\n    latitude=Decimal('27.717200000000000'),\n    longitude=Decimal('85.324000000000000'),\n    address='Kathmandu',\n    total_slots=1,\n    status='ONLINE',\n    hardware_info={}\n)\nslot = StationSlot.objects.create(station=station, slot_number=1, status='OCCUPIED', battery_level=50, slot_metadata={})\npb = PowerBank.objects.create(\n    serial_number=f'OPTPB{suffix}',\n    model='Standard',\n    capacity_mah=10000,\n    status='RENTED',\n    battery_level=50,\n    current_station=station,\n    current_slot=slot,\n    hardware_info={}\n)\nrental = Rental.objects.create(\n    user=user,\n    station=station,\n    slot=slot,\n    package=pkg,\n    power_bank=pb,\n    rental_code=f'O{suffix[-9:]}',\n    status='ACTIVE',\n    payment_status='PENDING',\n    started_at=now - timedelta(minutes=10),\n    due_at=now + timedelta(minutes=50),\n    amount_paid=Decimal('0.00'),\n    overdue_amount=Decimal('77.25')\n)\nslot.current_rental = rental\nslot.save(update_fields=['current_rental', 'updated_at'])\n\nprint(json.dumps({'rental_id': str(rental.id), 'overdue_amount': str(rental.overdue_amount)}, separators=(',', ':')))\n'@\n$seed | docker exec -i cg-api-local sh -lc \"cd /application && uv run manage.py shell\"","workdir":"e:\\Companies\\DEVALAYA\\ChargeGhar\\StationBackend\\UserBackend","timeout_ms":120000}
```

```text
31:\t
32:\tprint_step() {
33:\t    echo -e "${BLUE}[STEP]${NC} $1"
34:\t}
35:\t
36:\t# Check if running as root
37:\tif [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
38:\t   print_error "This script must be run as root"
39:\t   exit 1
40:\tfi
41:\t
42:\t# Create and navigate to project directory
43:\tif [[ ! -d "$PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
44:\t    mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR"
45:\tfi
46:\tcd "$PROJECT_DIR"
47:\t
48:\t# Update repository
49:\tprint_step "Repository Management..."
50:\tif [[ -d ".git" ]]; then
51:\t    # Show current git status
52:\t    echo ""
53:\t    echo -e "${YELLOW}Current Git Status:${NC}"
54:\t    echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
55:\t    echo "Last commit: $(git log -1 --oneline)"
56:\t    echo ""
57:\t    
58:\t    # Show available branches
59:\t    echo -e "${YELLOW}Available branches:${NC}"
60:\t    git branch -a | grep -E "(main|master|develop|staging)" | head -5
61:\t    echo ""
62:\t    
63:\t    # Interactive menu for git operations
64:\t    echo -e "${BLUE}Git Update Options:${NC}"
65:\t    echo "1. ðŸ”„ Pull latest from current branch ($(git branch --show-current))"
66:\t    echo "2. ðŸ”€ Switch to main branch and pull"
67:\t    echo "3. ðŸ”€ Switch to different branch"
68:\t    echo "4. ðŸ—‘ï¸  Hard reset to remote (discards local changes)"
69:\t    echo "5. ðŸ’¾ Stash changes and pull"
70:\t    echo "6. â­ï¸  Skip git update (use current code)"
71:\t    echo ""
72:\t    
73:\t    read -p "Select option (1-6): " git_choice
74:\t    
75:\t    case $git_choice in
76:\t        1)
77:\t            print_step "Pulling latest from current branch..."
78:\t            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)"
79:\t            ;;
80:\t        2)
81:\t            print_step "Switching to main branch and pulling..."
82:\t            git stash push -m "Auto-stash before switching to main $(date)" || true
83:\t            git checkout "$BRANCH" || git checkout -b "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
84:\t            git pull origin "$BRANCH"
85:\t            ;;
86:\t        3)
87:\t            echo ""
88:\t            echo -e "${YELLOW}Available branches:${NC}"
89:\t            git branch -a | sed 's/remotes\/origin\///' | grep -v HEAD | sort | uniq | nl
90:\t            echo ""
91:\t            read -p "Enter branch name: " custom_branch
92:\t            if [[ -n "$custom_branch" ]]; then
93:\t                print_step "Switching to branch: $custom_branch"
94:\t                git stash push -m "Auto-stash before switching to $custom_branch $(date)" || true
95:\t                git checkout "$custom_branch" || git checkout -b "$custom_branch" "origin/$custom_branch"
96:\t                git pull origin "$custom_branch" || true
97:\t                BRANCH="$custom_branch"  # Update branch variable
98:\t            else
99:\t                print_error "No branch specified, using current branch"
100:\t            fi
101:\t            ;;
102:\t        4)
103:\t            print_step "Hard reset to remote (WARNING: This will discard local changes)..."
104:\t            read -p "Are you sure? Type 'YES' to confirm: " confirm
105:\t            if [[ "$confirm" == "YES" ]]; then
106:\t                git fetch origin
107:\t                git reset --hard "origin/$(git branch --show-current)"
108:\t                print_status "Hard reset completed"
109:\t            else
110:\t                print_status "Hard reset cancelled"
111:\t            fi
112:\t            ;;
113:\t        5)
114:\t            print_step "Stashing changes and pulling..."
115:\t            git stash push -m "Manual stash before deployment $(date)"
116:\t            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)"
117:\t            ;;
118:\t        6)
119:\t            print_status "Skipping git update, using current code"
120:\t            ;;
121:\t        *)
122:\t            print_error "Invalid choice, defaulting to pull current branch"
123:\t            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)" || true
124:\t            ;;
125:\t    esac
126:\t    
127:\t    print_status "Repository updated"
128:\telse
129:\t    print_step "Cloning repository for first time..."
130:\t    git clone "$REPO_URL" .
131:\t    git checkout "$BRANCH"
132:\t    print_status "Repository cloned"
133:\tfi
134:\t
135:\t# Configure environment for production
136:\tprint_step "Configuring production environment..."
137:\tcp .env .env.backup 2>/dev/null || true
138:\tsed -i 's|ENVIRONMENT=local|ENVIRONMENT=production|' .env
139:\tsed -i 's|DJANGO_DEBUG=true|DJANGO_DEBUG=false|' .env
140:\tsed -i 's|CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=true|CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=false|' .env
141:\tsed -i 's|CELERY_TASK_EAGER_PROPAGATES=true|CELERY_TASK_EAGER_PROPAGATES=false|' .env
142:\tsed -i 's|CELERY_TASK_IGNORE_RESULT=true|CELERY_TASK_IGNORE_RESULT=false|' .env
143:\t# Update service names to match production docker-compose
144:\tsed -i 's|POSTGRES_HOST=pgbouncer|POSTGRES_HOST=db|' .env
145:\tsed -i 's|POSTGRES_HOST=.*|POSTGRES_HOST=db|' .env
146:\tsed -i 's|REDIS_HOST=.*|REDIS_HOST=redis|' .env
147:\tsed -i 's|RABBITMQ_HOST=.*|RABBITMQ_HOST=rabbitmq|' .env
148:\tsed -i 's|BASE_URL=http://localhost:8010|BASE_URL=https://main.chargeghar.com|' .env
149:\t# Update frontend URL for production redirects
150:\tsed -i 's|FRONTEND_URL=.*|FRONTEND_URL=https://chargeghar.app|' .env
151:\t# Update CORS and CSRF origins for production
152:\tsed -i 's|CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=.*|CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://main.chargeghar.com,https://chargeghar.app,https://app.chargeghar.com,http://localhost,http://213.210.21.113:8081|' .env
153:\tsed -i 's|CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS=.*|CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS=https://main.chargeghar.com,https://chargeghar.app,https://app.chargeghar.com,http://localhost,http://213.210.21.113:8081|' .env
154:\tprint_status "Environment configured"
155:\t
156:\t# Create directories
157:\tmkdir -p logs staticfiles backups
158:\t
159:\t# Stop existing containers and clean up
160:\tprint_step "Stopping containers and cleaning up..."
161:\t# IMPORTANT: Removed --volumes flag to preserve database data
162:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" down --remove-orphans || true
163:\t
164:\t# Also stop old containers from previous project name (one-time migration)
165:\tif docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}" | grep -q "^cg-"; then
166:\t    print_step "Removing old cg-* containers from previous project..."
167:\t    docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}" | grep "^cg-" | xargs -r docker rm -f || true
168:\tfi
169:\t
170:\t# Kill any containers using port 8010
171:\tprint_step "Checking for port conflicts..."
172:\tAPI_PORT=$(grep "API_PORT" .env | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d ' ')
173:\tPORT_TO_CHECK=${API_PORT:-8010}
174:\t
175:\t# Find and stop containers using the port
176:\tCONFLICTING_CONTAINERS=$(docker ps --filter "publish=$PORT_TO_CHECK" --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null || true)
177:\tif [[ -n "$CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS" ]]; then
178:\t    print_step "Found containers using port $PORT_TO_CHECK, stopping them..."
179:\t    echo "$CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS" | while read container; do
180:\t        echo "Stopping container: $container"
181:\t        docker stop "$container" || true
182:\t        docker rm "$container" || true
183:\t    done
184:\tfi
185:\t
186:\t# Also check for any remaining processes on the port
187:\tPROCESS_ON_PORT=$(netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ":$PORT_TO_CHECK " | awk '{print $7}' | cut -d'/' -f1 || true)
188:\tif [[ -n "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" && "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" != "-" ]]; then
189:\t    print_step "Killing process $PROCESS_ON_PORT using port $PORT_TO_CHECK..."
190:\t    kill -9 "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" 2>/dev/null || true
191:\tfi
192:\t
193:\tdocker system prune -f
194:\tprint_status "Cleanup completed"
195:\t
196:\t# Build and start services
197:\tprint_step "Building and starting services..."
198:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" build --no-cache
199:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" up -d
200:\tprint_status "Services started"
201:\t
202:\t# Wait for services to be ready
203:\tprint_step "Waiting for services to initialize..."
204:\tsleep 30
205:\t
206:\t# Check migration status
207:\tprint_step "Checking migration status..."
208:\tif docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations | grep -q "Exit 0"; then
209:\t    print_status "Migrations completed successfully"
210:\telif docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations | grep -q "Exit"; then
211:\t    print_error "Migrations failed! Checking logs..."
212:\t    docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" logs migrations
213:\t    exit 1
214:\telse
215:\t    print_step "Migrations still running, waiting..."
216:\t    sleep 30
217:\tfi
218:\t
219:\t# Ensure static files are collected
220:\tprint_step "Collecting static files..."
221:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T api python manage.py collectstatic --noinput || true
222:\t
223:\t# Show container status
224:\tprint_step "Container Status:"
225:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps
226:\t
227:\t# Check for any failed services (excluding successful one-time services)
228:\tprint_step "Checking for failed services..."
229:\tFAILED_SERVICES=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps --filter "status=exited" --format "table {{.Service}}\t{{.Status}}" | grep -v "SERVICE" | grep -v "Exit 0" | grep -v "migrations" | grep -v "collectstatic" || true)
230:\tif [[ -n "$FAILED_SERVICES" ]]; then
231:\t    print_error "Some services failed:"
232:\t    echo "$FAILED_SERVICES"
233:\t    print_step "Showing logs for failed services..."
234:\t    docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" logs --tail=50
235:\t    exit 1
236:\telse
237:\t    print_status "All services are running successfully!"
238:\t    
239:\t    # Check one-time services completed successfully
240:\t    MIGRATION_STATUS=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations --format "{{.Status}}" | grep "Exit 0" || echo "")
241:\t    COLLECTSTATIC_STATUS=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps collectstatic --format "{{.Status}}" | grep "Exit 0" || echo "")
242:\t    
243:\t    if [[ -n "$MIGRATION_STATUS" ]]; then
244:\t        print_status "âœ… Database migrations completed successfully"
245:\t    fi
246:\t    
247:\t    if [[ -n "$COLLECTSTATIC_STATUS" ]]; then
248:\t        print_status "âœ… Static files collection completed successfully"
249:\t    fi
250:\tfi
251:\t
252:\t# Auto-load fixtures (smart selection based on deployment state)
253:\tprint_step "Loading fixtures..."
254:\tif [[ -f "load-fixtures-safe.sh" ]] && [[ -f "load-fixtures.sh" ]]; then
255:\t    # Check if database already has data (indicating existing deployment)
256:\t    DB_HAS_DATA=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T api python manage.py shell -c "
257:\tfrom django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
258:\tfrom api.stations.models import Station
259:\tUser = get_user_model()
260:\tuser_count = User.objects.count()
261:\tstation_count = Station.objects.count()
262:\tif user_count > 0 or station_count > 0:
263:\t    print('EXISTING')
264:\telse:
265:\t    print('NEW')
266:\t" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r' | tail -1)
267:\t
268:\t    if [[ "$DB_HAS_DATA" == "EXISTING" ]]; then
269:\t        print_status "Existing deployment detected, using safe fixtures loader..."
270:\t        chmod +x load-fixtures-safe.sh
271:\t        ./load-fixtures-safe.sh
272:\t        print_status "Fixtures loaded safely (existing deployment)"
273:\t    else
274:\t        print_status "New deployment detected, using full fixtures loader..."
275:\t        chmod +x load-fixtures.sh
276:\t        ./load-fixtures.sh
277:\t        print_status "Fixtures loaded (new deployment)"
278:\t    fi
279:\telif [[ -f "load-fixtures-safe.sh" ]]; then
280:\t    print_warning "Only safe fixtures loader available, using it..."
281:\t    chmod +x load-fixtures-safe.sh
282:\t    ./load-fixtures-safe.sh
283:\t    print_status "Fixtures loaded safely"
284:\telif [[ -f "load-fixtures.sh" ]]; then
285:\t    print_warning "Only regular fixtures loader available, using it..."
286:\t    chmod +x load-fixtures.sh
287:\t    ./load-fixtures.sh
288:\t    print_status "Fixtures loaded"
289:\telse
290:\t    print_error "No fixtures loader found, skipping fixture loading"
291:\tfi
292:\t
293:\t# Final status
294:\tAPI_PORT=$(grep "API_PORT" .env | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d ' ')
295:\tSERVER_IP=$(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}')
296:\t
297:\tprint_step ""
298:\tprint_status "ðŸŽ‰ ChargeGhar Deployment Completed!"
299:\tprint_status "========================================"
300:\tprint_status "API URL: https://main.chargeghar.com"
301:\tprint_status "API Documentation: https://main.chargeghar.com/docs/"
302:\tprint_status "Admin Panel: https://main.chargeghar.com/admin/"
303:\tprint_status ""
304:\tprint_status "Use 'python3 mgr.py' for management tasks"
305:\tprint_status ""

```

```text
1:\t#!/bin/bash
2:\t
3:\t# PowerBank Django Production Deployment Script
4:\t# Clean deployment focused on containers and fixtures
5:\t
6:\tset -e  # Exit on any error
7:\t
8:\techo "ðŸš€ PowerBank Django Production Deployment"
9:\techo "=========================================="
10:\t
11:\t# Configuration
12:\tPROJECT_DIR="/opt/User-Backend"
13:\tREPO_URL="https://github.com/ChargeGhar/User-Backend.git"
14:\tBRANCH="main"
15:\tDOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE="docker-compose.prod.yml"
16:\t
17:\t# Colors for output
18:\tRED='\033[0;31m'
19:\tGREEN='\033[0;32m'
20:\tYELLOW='\033[1;33m'
21:\tBLUE='\033[0;34m'
22:\tNC='\033[0m' # No Color
23:\t
24:\tprint_status() {
25:\t    echo -e "${GREEN}[âœ“]${NC} $1"
26:\t}
27:\t
28:\tprint_error() {
29:\t    echo -e "${RED}[âœ—]${NC} $1"
30:\t}
31:\t
32:\tprint_step() {
33:\t    echo -e "${BLUE}[STEP]${NC} $1"
34:\t}
35:\t
36:\t# Check if running as root
37:\tif [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
38:\t   print_error "This script must be run as root"
39:\t   exit 1
40:\tfi
41:\t
42:\t# Create and navigate to project directory
43:\tif [[ ! -d "$PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
44:\t    mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR"
45:\tfi
46:\tcd "$PROJECT_DIR"
47:\t
48:\t# Update repository
49:\tprint_step "Repository Management..."
50:\tif [[ -d ".git" ]]; then
51:\t    # Show current git status
52:\t    echo ""
53:\t    echo -e "${YELLOW}Current Git Status:${NC}"
54:\t    echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
55:\t    echo "Last commit: $(git log -1 --oneline)"
56:\t    echo ""
57:\t    
58:\t    # Show available branches
59:\t    echo -e "${YELLOW}Available branches:${NC}"
60:\t    git branch -a | grep -E "(main|master|develop|staging)" | head -5
61:\t    echo ""
62:\t    
63:\t    # Interactive menu for git operations
64:\t    echo -e "${BLUE}Git Update Options:${NC}"
65:\t    echo "1. ðŸ”„ Pull latest from current branch ($(git branch --show-current))"
66:\t    echo "2. ðŸ”€ Switch to main branch and pull"
67:\t    echo "3. ðŸ”€ Switch to different branch"
68:\t    echo "4. ðŸ—‘ï¸  Hard reset to remote (discards local changes)"
69:\t    echo "5. ðŸ’¾ Stash changes and pull"
70:\t    echo "6. â­ï¸  Skip git update (use current code)"
71:\t    echo ""
72:\t    
73:\t    read -p "Select option (1-6): " git_choice
74:\t    
75:\t    case $git_choice in
76:\t        1)
77:\t            print_step "Pulling latest from current branch..."
78:\t            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)"
79:\t            ;;
80:\t        2)
81:\t            print_step "Switching to main branch and pulling..."
82:\t            git stash push -m "Auto-stash before switching to main $(date)" || true
83:\t            git checkout "$BRANCH" || git checkout -b "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
84:\t            git pull origin "$BRANCH"
85:\t            ;;
86:\t        3)
87:\t            echo ""
88:\t            echo -e "${YELLOW}Available branches:${NC}"
89:\t            git branch -a | sed 's/remotes\/origin\///' | grep -v HEAD | sort | uniq | nl
90:\t            echo ""
91:\t            read -p "Enter branch name: " custom_branch
92:\t            if [[ -n "$custom_branch" ]]; then
93:\t                print_step "Switching to branch: $custom_branch"
94:\t                git stash push -m "Auto-stash before switching to $custom_branch $(date)" || true
95:\t                git checkout "$custom_branch" || git checkout -b "$custom_branch" "origin/$custom_branch"
96:\t                git pull origin "$custom_branch" || true
97:\t                BRANCH="$custom_branch"  # Update branch variable
98:\t            else
99:\t                print_error "No branch specified, using current branch"
100:\t            fi
101:\t            ;;
102:\t        4)
103:\t            print_step "Hard reset to remote (WARNING: This will discard local changes)..."
104:\t            read -p "Are you sure? Type 'YES' to confirm: " confirm
105:\t            if [[ "$confirm" == "YES" ]]; then
106:\t                git fetch origin
107:\t                git reset --hard "origin/$(git branch --show-current)"
108:\t                print_status "Hard reset completed"
109:\t            else
110:\t                print_status "Hard reset cancelled"
111:\t            fi
112:\t            ;;
113:\t        5)
114:\t            print_step "Stashing changes and pulling..."
115:\t            git stash push -m "Manual stash before deployment $(date)"
116:\t            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)"
117:\t            ;;
118:\t        6)
119:\t            print_status "Skipping git update, using current code"
120:\t            ;;
121:\t        *)
122:\t            print_error "Invalid choice, defaulting to pull current branch"
123:\t            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)" || true
124:\t            ;;
125:\t    esac
126:\t    
127:\t    print_status "Repository updated"
128:\telse
129:\t    print_step "Cloning repository for first time..."
130:\t    git clone "$REPO_URL" .
131:\t    git checkout "$BRANCH"
132:\t    print_status "Repository cloned"
133:\tfi
134:\t
135:\t# Configure environment for production
136:\tprint_step "Configuring production environment..."
137:\tcp .env .env.backup 2>/dev/null || true
138:\tif [[ ! -f ".env.prod" ]]; then
139:\t    print_error ".env.prod not found in $PROJECT_DIR"
140:\t    exit 1
141:\tfi
142:\tcp .env.prod .env
143:\tprint_status "Environment configured using .env.prod"
144:\t
145:\t# Create directories
146:\tmkdir -p logs staticfiles backups
147:\t
148:\t# Stop existing containers and clean up
149:\tprint_step "Stopping containers and cleaning up..."
150:\t# IMPORTANT: Removed --volumes flag to preserve database data
151:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" down --remove-orphans || true
152:\t
153:\t# Also stop old containers from previous project name (one-time migration)
154:\tif docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}" | grep -q "^cg-"; then
155:\t    print_step "Removing old cg-* containers from previous project..."
156:\t    docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}" | grep "^cg-" | xargs -r docker rm -f || true
157:\tfi
158:\t
159:\t# Kill any containers using port 8010
160:\tprint_step "Checking for port conflicts..."
161:\tAPI_PORT=$(grep "API_PORT" .env | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d ' ')
162:\tPORT_TO_CHECK=${API_PORT:-8010}
163:\t
164:\t# Find and stop containers using the port
165:\tCONFLICTING_CONTAINERS=$(docker ps --filter "publish=$PORT_TO_CHECK" --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null || true)
166:\tif [[ -n "$CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS" ]]; then
167:\t    print_step "Found containers using port $PORT_TO_CHECK, stopping them..."
168:\t    echo "$CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS" | while read container; do
169:\t        echo "Stopping container: $container"
170:\t        docker stop "$container" || true
171:\t        docker rm "$container" || true
172:\t    done
173:\tfi
174:\t
175:\t# Also check for any remaining processes on the port
176:\tPROCESS_ON_PORT=$(netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ":$PORT_TO_CHECK " | awk '{print $7}' | cut -d'/' -f1 || true)
177:\tif [[ -n "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" && "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" != "-" ]]; then
178:\t    print_step "Killing process $PROCESS_ON_PORT using port $PORT_TO_CHECK..."
179:\t    kill -9 "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" 2>/dev/null || true
180:\tfi
181:\t
182:\tdocker system prune -f
183:\tprint_status "Cleanup completed"
184:\t
185:\t# Build and start services
186:\tprint_step "Building and starting services..."
187:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" build --no-cache
188:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" up -d
189:\tprint_status "Services started"
190:\t
191:\t# Wait for services to be ready
192:\tprint_step "Waiting for services to initialize..."
193:\tsleep 30
194:\t
195:\t# Check migration status
196:\tprint_step "Checking migration status..."
197:\tif docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations | grep -q "Exit 0"; then
198:\t    print_status "Migrations completed successfully"
199:\telif docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations | grep -q "Exit"; then
200:\t    print_error "Migrations failed! Checking logs..."
201:\t    docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" logs migrations
202:\t    exit 1
203:\telse
204:\t    print_step "Migrations still running, waiting..."
205:\t    sleep 30
206:\tfi
207:\t
208:\t# Ensure static files are collected
209:\tprint_step "Collecting static files..."
210:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T api python manage.py collectstatic --noinput || true
211:\t
212:\t# Show container status
213:\tprint_step "Container Status:"
214:\tdocker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps
215:\t
216:\t# Check for any failed services (excluding successful one-time services)
217:\tprint_step "Checking for failed services..."
218:\tFAILED_SERVICES=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps --filter "status=exited" --format "table {{.Service}}\t{{.Status}}" | grep -v "SERVICE" | grep -v "Exit 0" | grep -v "migrations" | grep -v "collectstatic" || true)
219:\tif [[ -n "$FAILED_SERVICES" ]]; then
220:\t    print_error "Some services failed:"
221:\t    echo "$FAILED_SERVICES"
222:\t    print_step "Showing logs for failed services..."
223:\t    docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" logs --tail=50
224:\t    exit 1
225:\telse
226:\t    print_status "All services are running successfully!"
227:\t    
228:\t    # Check one-time services completed successfully
229:\t    MIGRATION_STATUS=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations --format "{{.Status}}" | grep "Exit 0" || echo "")
230:\t    COLLECTSTATIC_STATUS=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps collectstatic --format "{{.Status}}" | grep "Exit 0" || echo "")
231:\t    
232:\t    if [[ -n "$MIGRATION_STATUS" ]]; then
233:\t        print_status "âœ… Database migrations completed successfully"
234:\t    fi
235:\t    
236:\t    if [[ -n "$COLLECTSTATIC_STATUS" ]]; then
237:\t        print_status "âœ… Static files collection completed successfully"
238:\t    fi
239:\tfi
240:\t
241:\t# Auto-load fixtures (smart selection based on deployment state)
242:\tprint_step "Loading fixtures..."
243:\tif [[ -f "load-fixtures-safe.sh" ]] && [[ -f "load-fixtures.sh" ]]; then
244:\t    # Check if database already has data (indicating existing deployment)
245:\t    DB_HAS_DATA=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T api python manage.py shell -c "
246:\tfrom django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
247:\tfrom api.stations.models import Station
248:\tUser = get_user_model()
249:\tuser_count = User.objects.count()
250:\tstation_count = Station.objects.count()
251:\tif user_count > 0 or station_count > 0:
252:\t    print('EXISTING')
253:\telse:
254:\t    print('NEW')
255:\t" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r' | tail -1)
256:\t
257:\t    if [[ "$DB_HAS_DATA" == "EXISTING" ]]; then
258:\t        print_status "Existing deployment detected, using safe fixtures loader..."
259:\t        chmod +x load-fixtures-safe.sh
260:\t        ./load-fixtures-safe.sh
261:\t        print_status "Fixtures loaded safely (existing deployment)"
262:\t    else
263:\t        print_status "New deployment detected, using full fixtures loader..."
264:\t        chmod +x load-fixtures.sh
265:\t        ./load-fixtures.sh
266:\t        print_status "Fixtures loaded (new deployment)"
267:\t    fi
268:\telif [[ -f "load-fixtures-safe.sh" ]]; then
269:\t    print_warning "Only safe fixtures loader available, using it..."
270:\t    chmod +x load-fixtures-safe.sh
271:\t    ./load-fixtures-safe.sh
272:\t    print_status "Fixtures loaded safely"
273:\telif [[ -f "load-fixtures.sh" ]]; then
274:\t    print_warning "Only regular fixtures loader available, using it..."
275:\t    chmod +x load-fixtures.sh
276:\t    ./load-fixtures.sh
277:\t    print_status "Fixtures loaded"
278:\telse
279:\t    print_error "No fixtures loader found, skipping fixture loading"
280:\tfi
281:\t
282:\t# Final status
283:\tAPI_PORT=$(grep "API_PORT" .env | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d ' ')
284:\tSERVER_IP=$(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}')
285:\t
286:\tprint_step ""
287:\tprint_status "ðŸŽ‰ ChargeGhar Deployment Completed!"
288:\tprint_status "========================================"
289:\tprint_status "API URL: https://main.chargeghar.com"
290:\tprint_status "API Documentation: https://main.chargeghar.com/docs/"
291:\tprint_status "Admin Panel: https://main.chargeghar.com/admin/"
292:\tprint_status ""
293:\tprint_status "Use 'python3 mgr.py' for management tasks"
294:\tprint_status ""
```

```text
58:\t    def run_command(self, command, description="", show_output=True):
59:\t        """Run a shell command with nice formatting"""
60:\t        if description:
61:\t            print(f"{Colors.BLUE}[RUNNING]{Colors.ENDC} {description}...")
62:\t        
63:\t        try:
64:\t            if show_output:
65:\t                result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True, cwd=self.project_dir)
66:\t                return result.returncode == 0
67:\t            else:
68:\t                result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=self.project_dir)
69:\t                return result.stdout.strip()
70:\t        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
71:\t            print(f"{Colors.RED}[ERROR]{Colors.ENDC} Command failed: {e}")
72:\t            return False
73:\t
74:\t    def clear_port_conflicts(self):
75:\t        """Clear any port conflicts before deployment"""
76:\t        print(f"{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ” Checking for port conflicts...{Colors.ENDC}")
77:\t        
78:\t        # Get API port from .env
79:\t        try:
80:\t            with open('.env', 'r') as f:
81:\t                for line in f:
82:\t                    if line.startswith('API_PORT='):
83:\t                        port = line.split('=')[1].strip()
84:\t                        break
85:\t                else:
86:\t                    port = "8010"  # default
87:\t        except:
88:\t            port = "8010"
89:\t        
90:\t        # Find containers using the port
91:\t        conflicting = self.run_command(f'docker ps --filter "publish={port}" --format "{{{{.Names}}}}"', show_output=False)
92:\t        if conflicting:
93:\t            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Found containers using port {port}:{Colors.ENDC}")
94:\t            for container in conflicting.split('\n'):
95:\t                if container.strip():
96:\t                    print(f"  Stopping: {container}")
97:\t                    self.run_command(f"docker stop {container}", show_output=False)
98:\t                    self.run_command(f"docker rm {container}", show_output=False)
99:\t        
100:\t        # Check for processes on port
101:\t        process_check = self.run_command(f'netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ":{port} "', show_output=False)
102:\t        if process_check:
103:\t            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Port {port} is still in use, attempting to free it...{Colors.ENDC}")
104:\t            # Kill docker-proxy processes if any
105:\t            self.run_command("pkill -f docker-proxy", show_output=False)
106:\t        
107:\t        print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Port conflicts cleared{Colors.ENDC}")
108:\t
109:\t    def restart_services(self):
110:\t        """Restart all services"""
111:\t        print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}ðŸ”„ Restarting PowerBank Services...{Colors.ENDC}")
112:\t        
113:\t        # Clear port conflicts first
114:\t        self.clear_port_conflicts()
115:\t        
116:\t        # Stop and start instead of restart to avoid port issues
117:\t        self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down", "Stopping containers")
118:\t        time.sleep(3)
119:\t        success = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} up -d", "Starting containers")
120:\t        

```

</details>

```text
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
```
```text
x-common: &common
  env_file:
    - .env
  logging:
    driver: "json-file"
    options:
      max-size: "10m"
      max-file: "3"
  networks:
    - cg_main
  restart: unless-stopped

services:
  api:
    <<: *common
    image: ghcr.io/chargeghar/user-backend:latest
    container_name: cg-api
    ports:
      - "${API_PORT:-8010}:80"
    environment:
      WORKERS: ${WORKERS:-4}
      THREADS: ${THREADS:-16}
      ENVIRONMENT: production
    command: sh -c "python manage.py collectstatic --noinput && python -m gunicorn api.web.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:80 --workers ${WORKERS:-4} --threads ${THREADS:-16} --timeout 480"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      rabbitmq:
        condition: service_healthy
      migrations:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      collectstatic:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:80/api/app/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 120s
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1G
        reservations:
          memory: 512M
    volumes:
      - static_volume:/application/staticfiles

  db:
    <<: *common
    image: postgres:15
    container_name: cg-db
    environment:
      POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS: --auth=scram-sha-256
    volumes:
      - cg_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER} -d ${POSTGRES_DB}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M
        reservations:
          memory: 256M
    command: postgres -c shared_preload_libraries=pg_stat_statements -c pg_stat_statements.track=all

  redis:
    <<: *common
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: cg-redis
    ports:
      - "6380:6379"
    volumes:
      - cg_redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "-a", "${REDIS_PASSWORD}", "ping"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 128M
        reservations:
          memory: 64M
    command: redis-server --maxmemory 64mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD}

  rabbitmq:
    <<: *common
    image: rabbitmq:3.13-management-alpine
    container_name: cg-rabbitmq
    environment:
        RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER:-powerbank}
        RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS}
    volumes:
      - cg_rabbitmq_data:/var/lib/rabbitmq
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "rabbitmq-diagnostics", "-q", "ping"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 60s
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 256M
        reservations:
          memory: 128M

  celery:
    <<: *common
    image: ghcr.io/chargeghar/user-backend:latest
    container_name: cg-celery
    command: python -m celery -A tasks.app worker --loglevel=INFO --concurrency=2 --max-tasks-per-child=100 --prefetch-multiplier=1
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M
        reservations:
          memory: 256M

  celery_beat:
    <<: *common
    image: ghcr.io/chargeghar/user-backend:latest
    container_name: cg-celery-beat
    command: python -m celery -A tasks.app beat --loglevel=INFO --schedule=/tmp/celerybeat-schedule
    depends_on:
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      rabbitmq:
        condition: service_healthy
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 128M
        reservations:
          memory: 64M

  migrations:
    <<: *common
    image: ghcr.io/chargeghar/user-backend:latest
    container_name: cg-migrations
    command: sh -c "sleep 15 && python manage.py migrate"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: "no"

  collectstatic:
    <<: *common
    image: ghcr.io/chargeghar/user-backend:latest
    container_name: cg-collectstatic
    command: sh -c "sleep 15 && python manage.py collectstatic --noinput"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    volumes:
      - static_volume:/application/staticfiles
    restart: "no"

networks:
  cg_main:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  cg_postgres_data:
  cg_redis_data:
  cg_rabbitmq_data:
  static_volume:
  celery_beat_data:

```

</details>

```text
from os import getenv

from api.config.application import TIME_ZONE

broker_url = getenv("CELERY_BROKER_URL", "redis://localhost:6379/0")
result_backend = getenv("CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND", "redis://localhost:6379/0")
```

```text
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from os import getenv
from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

USE_REDIS_FOR_CACHE = getenv("USE_REDIS_FOR_CACHE", default="true").lower() == "true"
REDIS_URL = getenv("REDIS_URL", default="redis://localhost:6379/0")
REDIS_PASSWORD = getenv("REDIS_PASSWORD", "")

CACHES: dict[str, Any] = {}

if USE_REDIS_FOR_CACHE:
    logger.debug("Using Redis for cache")
    CACHES["default"] = {
        "BACKEND": "django_redis.cache.RedisCache",
        "LOCATION": REDIS_URL,
        "OPTIONS": {
            "CLIENT_CLASS": "django_redis.client.DefaultClient",
            **({"PASSWORD": REDIS_PASSWORD} if REDIS_PASSWORD else {}),
        },
    }

    # Don't test cache connection during startup to avoid blocking Django initialization
    # The cache will be tested when first used
    logger.debug("Redis cache configured - connection will be tested on first use")
else:
    logger.warning("Using dummy cache")
    CACHES["default"] = {
        "BACKEND": "django.core.cache.backends.dummy.DummyCache",
    }
```

```text
from __future__ import annotations
```

```text
import os

from celery import Celery

# Set up Django before any imports that might use Django models
os.environ.setdefault("DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", "api.config.settings")

import django
django.setup()

from api.config import celery as config

app = Celery("main")
app.config_from_object(config)

# Auto-discover tasks from all apps
app.autodiscover_tasks(
    [
        "api.user.auth",
        "api.user.stations",
        "api.user.payments",
        "api.user.points",
        "api.user.notifications",
        "api.user.rentals",
        "api.admin",
        "api.user.content",
        "api.user.social",
        "api.user.promotions",
        "api.user.system",
        "api.user.advertisements",
    ]
)

# Configure task routes for better organization
app.conf.task_routes = {
    # User-related tasks
    "api.user.auth.tasks.*": {"queue": "users"},
    # Station-related tasks
    "api.user.stations.tasks.*": {"queue": "stations"},
    # Payment-related tasks (high priority)
    "api.user.payments.tasks.*": {"queue": "payments"},
    # Points and referral tasks
    "api.user.points.tasks.*": {"queue": "points"},
    # Notification tasks (high priority)
    "api.user.notifications.tasks.*": {"queue": "notifications"},
    # Rental tasks (critical)
    "api.user.rentals.tasks.*": {"queue": "rentals"},
    # Admin and analytics tasks
    "api.admin.tasks.*": {"queue": "admin"},
    # System tasks (app updates, health checks)
    "api.user.system.tasks.*": {"queue": "system"},
    # Low priority tasks
    "api.user.content.tasks.*": {"queue": "low_priority"},
    "api.user.social.tasks.*": {"queue": "low_priority"},
    "api.user.promotions.tasks.*": {"queue": "low_priority"},
    # Advertisement tasks
    "advertisements.*": {"queue": "advertisements"},
}

# Configure periodic tasks
app.conf.beat_schedule = {
    # Critical system tasks (every minute)
    "check-overdue-rentals": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.check_overdue_rentals",
        "schedule": 60.0,  # Every 1 minute (increased frequency for real-time updates)
    },
    "check-offline-stations": {
        "task": "api.user.stations.tasks.check_offline_stations",
        "schedule": 300.0,  # Every 5 minutes
    },
    # Important tasks (every 15 minutes)
    "send-rental-reminders": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.send_rental_reminders",
        "schedule": 900.0,  # Every 15 minutes
    },
    "expire-payment-intents": {
        "task": "api.user.payments.tasks.expire_payment_intents",
        "schedule": 900.0,  # Every 15 minutes
    },
    # Regular tasks
    "calculate-overdue-charges": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.calculate_overdue_charges",
        "schedule": 60.0,  # Every 1 minute
    },
    "expire-old-referrals": {
        "task": "api.user.points.tasks.expire_old_referrals",
        "schedule": 3600.0,  # Every hour
    },
    # Daily tasks
    "cleanup-old-notifications": {
        "task": "api.user.notifications.tasks.cleanup_old_notifications_task",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
    },
    "cleanup-old-audit-logs": {
        "task": "api.user.auth.tasks.cleanup_expired_audit_logs",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
    },
    "auto-complete-abandoned-rentals": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.auto_complete_abandoned_rentals",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
    },
    "detect-rental-anomalies": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.detect_rental_anomalies",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
    },
    "deactivate-inactive-users": {
        "task": "api.user.auth.tasks.deactivate_inactive_users",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
    },
    "monitor-system-health": {
        "task": "api.admin.tasks.monitor_system_health",
        "schedule": 3600.0,  # Every hour
    },
    "send-admin-digest-report": {
        "task": "api.admin.tasks.send_admin_digest_report",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily at 8 AM
        "options": {"eta": "08:00"},
    },
    "backup-critical-data": {
        "task": "api.admin.tasks.backup_critical_data",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily at 1 AM
        "options": {"eta": "01:00"},
    },
    # Weekly tasks
    "cleanup-old-rental-data": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.cleanup_old_rental_data",
        "schedule": 604800.0,  # Weekly
    },
    "cleanup-old-points-transactions": {
        "task": "api.user.points.tasks.cleanup_old_transactions",
        "schedule": 604800.0,  # Weekly
    },
    "cleanup-old-payment-data": {
        "task": "api.user.payments.tasks.cleanup_old_payment_data",
        "schedule": 604800.0,  # Weekly
    },
    "cleanup-old-admin-logs": {
        "task": "api.admin.tasks.cleanup_old_admin_logs",
        "schedule": 604800.0,  # Weekly
    },
    "cleanup-old-system-logs": {
        "task": "api.admin.tasks.cleanup_old_system_logs",
        "schedule": 259200.0,  # Every 3 days
    },
    "cleanup-old-coupon-data": {
        "task": "api.user.promotions.tasks.cleanup_old_coupon_data",
        "schedule": 604800.0,  # Weekly
    },
    "cleanup-inactive-user-achievements": {
        "task": "api.user.social.tasks.cleanup_inactive_user_achievements",
        "schedule": 604800.0,  # Weekly
    },
    # Analytics tasks (daily at 2 AM)
    "generate-rental-analytics": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.generate_rental_analytics_report",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
        "options": {"eta": "02:00"},
    },
    "generate-admin-dashboard-report": {
        "task": "api.admin.tasks.generate_admin_dashboard_report",
        "schedule": 1800.0,  # Every 30 minutes (reduced from 5 minutes)
    },
    "generate-revenue-report": {
        "task": "api.admin.tasks.generate_revenue_report",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
        "options": {"eta": "04:00"},
    },
    # Maintenance tasks (weekly)
    "sync-wallet-balances": {
        "task": "api.user.payments.tasks.sync_wallet_balances",
        "schedule": 604800.0,  # Weekly
    },
    "sync-rental-payment-status": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.sync_rental_payment_status",
        "schedule": 604800.0,  # Weekly
    },
    # Optimization tasks (daily)
    "optimize-power-bank-distribution": {
        "task": "api.user.stations.tasks.optimize_power_bank_distribution",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
    },
    "update-station-popularity": {
        "task": "api.user.stations.tasks.update_station_popularity_score",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
    },
    "update-package-popularity": {
        "task": "api.user.rentals.tasks.update_rental_package_popularity",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily
    },
    # Advertisement tasks
    "auto-start-scheduled-ads": {
        "task": "advertisements.auto_start_scheduled_ads",
        "schedule": 3600.0,  # Every hour
        "options": {"queue": "advertisements"},
    },
    "auto-complete-finished-ads": {
        "task": "advertisements.auto_complete_finished_ads",
        "schedule": 3600.0,  # Every hour (offset by 15 minutes)
        "options": {"queue": "advertisements"},
    },
    "check-payment-pending-ads": {
        "task": "advertisements.check_payment_pending_ads",
        "schedule": 86400.0,  # Daily at 10 AM
        "options": {"queue": "advertisements", "eta": "10:00"},
    },
}

# Task execution settings
app.conf.task_time_limit = 300  # 5 minutes max per task
app.conf.task_soft_time_limit = 240  # 4 minutes soft limit
app.conf.worker_prefetch_multiplier = 1
app.conf.task_acks_late = True
app.conf.worker_disable_rate_limits = False
app.conf.worker_max_tasks_per_child = 100  # Restart worker after 100 tasks to prevent memory leaks

# Connection settings for better reliability
app.conf.broker_connection_retry_on_startup = True
app.conf.broker_connection_retry = True
app.conf.broker_connection_max_retries = 10

# Error handling
app.conf.task_reject_on_worker_lost = True
# task_ignore_result is set in api/config/celery.py via environment variable
app.conf.result_expires = 3600  # 1 hour

# Monitoring (disabled to reduce memory usage)
app.conf.worker_send_task_events = False
app.conf.task_send_sent_event = False

# Memory management
app.conf.worker_max_memory_per_child = 200000  # 200MB per worker process

# Production settings (simplified)
app.conf.worker_hijack_root_logger = False  # Don't interfere with Django logging

```
