```text
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
PowerBank Django Deployment Manager
Beautiful menu-driven management tool for PowerBank Django deployment
"""

import os
import sys
import subprocess
import time
from datetime import datetime

class Colors:
    HEADER = '\033[95m'
    BLUE = '\033[94m'
    CYAN = '\033[96m'
    GREEN = '\033[92m'
    YELLOW = '\033[93m'
    RED = '\033[91m'
    ENDC = '\033[0m'
    BOLD = '\033[1m'
    UNDERLINE = '\033[4m'

class PowerBankManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.compose_file = "docker-compose.prod.yml"
        self.project_dir = "/opt/powerbank"
        
    def print_header(self):
        print(f"\n{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}")
        print(f"{Colors.BOLD}{Colors.HEADER}ðŸš€ PowerBank Django Deployment Manager{Colors.ENDC}")
        print(f"{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}")
        print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Current Time: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}{Colors.ENDC}")
        print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Project Directory: {self.project_dir}{Colors.ENDC}")
        print(f"{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}\n")

    def print_menu(self):
        menu_items = [
            ("1", "ðŸ”„ Restart Services", "Restart all PowerBank containers"),
            ("2", "â¹ï¸  Stop Services", "Stop all PowerBank containers"),
            ("3", "ðŸ—‘ï¸  Delete All", "Stop and remove all containers, volumes, images"),
            ("4", "ðŸ“Š Service Status", "Show current status of all services"),
            ("5", "ðŸ“‹ View Logs", "View logs for all services or specific service"),
            ("6", "ðŸ”§ Load Fixtures", "Load sample data into the database"),
            ("7", "ðŸ¥ Health Check", "Check API health and connectivity"),
            ("8", "ðŸ”„ Full Redeploy", "Complete redeployment (git pull + rebuild)"),
            ("9", "ðŸ’¾ Database Backup", "Create database backup"),
            ("10", "ðŸ§¹ Clean Docker", "Clean unused Docker resources"),
            ("11", "ðŸ”“ Clear Port Conflicts", "Fix port conflicts (8010)"),
            ("0", "âŒ Exit", "Exit the manager")
        ]
        
        print(f"{Colors.BOLD}{Colors.BLUE}Available Actions:{Colors.ENDC}\n")
        for num, title, desc in menu_items:
            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}{num:>2}.{Colors.ENDC} {Colors.BOLD}{title:<20}{Colors.ENDC} - {desc}")
        print()

    def run_command(self, command, description="", show_output=True):
        """Run a shell command with nice formatting"""
        if description:
            print(f"{Colors.BLUE}[RUNNING]{Colors.ENDC} {description}...")
        
        try:
            if show_output:
                result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True, cwd=self.project_dir)
                return result.returncode == 0
            else:
                result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=self.project_dir)
                return result.stdout.strip()
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            print(f"{Colors.RED}[ERROR]{Colors.ENDC} Command failed: {e}")
            return False

    def clear_port_conflicts(self):
        """Clear any port conflicts before deployment"""
        print(f"{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ” Checking for port conflicts...{Colors.ENDC}")
        
        # Get API port from .env
        try:
            with open('.env', 'r') as f:
                for line in f:
                    if line.startswith('API_PORT='):
                        port = line.split('=')[1].strip()
                        break
                else:
                    port = "8010"  # default
        except:
            port = "8010"
        
        # Find containers using the port
        conflicting = self.run_command(f'docker ps --filter "publish={port}" --format "{{{{.Names}}}}"', show_output=False)
        if conflicting:
            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Found containers using port {port}:{Colors.ENDC}")
            for container in conflicting.split('\n'):
                if container.strip():
                    print(f"  Stopping: {container}")
                    self.run_command(f"docker stop {container}", show_output=False)
                    self.run_command(f"docker rm {container}", show_output=False)
        
        # Check for processes on port
        process_check = self.run_command(f'netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ":{port} "', show_output=False)
        if process_check:
            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Port {port} is still in use, attempting to free it...{Colors.ENDC}")
            # Kill docker-proxy processes if any
            self.run_command("pkill -f docker-proxy", show_output=False)
        
        print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Port conflicts cleared{Colors.ENDC}")

    def restart_services(self):
        """Restart all services"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}ðŸ”„ Restarting PowerBank Services...{Colors.ENDC}")
        
        # Clear port conflicts first
        self.clear_port_conflicts()
        
        # Stop and start instead of restart to avoid port issues
        self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down", "Stopping containers")
        time.sleep(3)
        success = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} up -d", "Starting containers")
        
        if success:
            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Services restarted successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
            time.sleep(5)
            self.show_status()
        else:
            print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Failed to restart services{Colors.ENDC}")

    def stop_services(self):
        """Stop all services"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}â¹ï¸ Stopping PowerBank Services...{Colors.ENDC}")
        
        # Clear port conflicts to ensure clean shutdown
        self.clear_port_conflicts()
        
        success = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down --remove-orphans", "Stopping containers")
        if success:
            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Services stopped successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
        else:
            print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Failed to stop services{Colors.ENDC}")

    def delete_all(self):
        """Delete all containers, volumes, and images"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.RED}ðŸ—‘ï¸ WARNING: This will delete ALL PowerBank data!{Colors.ENDC}")
        confirm = input(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Type 'DELETE' to confirm: {Colors.ENDC}")
        
        if confirm == "DELETE":
            print(f"{Colors.RED}Deleting all PowerBank resources...{Colors.ENDC}")
            commands = [
                f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down --remove-orphans --volumes",
                "docker system prune -af",
                "docker volume prune -f"
            ]
            
            for cmd in commands:
                self.run_command(cmd)
            
            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… All resources deleted!{Colors.ENDC}")
        else:
            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}âŒ Deletion cancelled{Colors.ENDC}")

    def show_status(self):
        """Show service status"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ“Š PowerBank Service Status:{Colors.ENDC}")
        self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} ps")

    def view_logs(self):
        """View logs with service selection"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ“‹ Available Services:{Colors.ENDC}")
        services = ["all", "api", "db", "redis", "rabbitmq", "celery"]
        
        for i, service in enumerate(services, 1):
            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}{i}.{Colors.ENDC} {service}")
        
        try:
            choice = int(input(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Select service (1-{len(services)}): {Colors.ENDC}"))
            if 1 <= choice <= len(services):
                service = services[choice - 1]
                if service == "all":
                    cmd = f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} logs --tail=50 -f"
                else:
                    cmd = f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} logs --tail=50 -f {service}"
                
                print(f"{Colors.BLUE}Showing logs for {service} (Press Ctrl+C to exit)...{Colors.ENDC}")
                self.run_command(cmd)
            else:
                print(f"{Colors.RED}Invalid choice{Colors.ENDC}")
        except (ValueError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Returning to menu...{Colors.ENDC}")

    def load_fixtures(self):
        """Load fixtures"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ”§ Loading PowerBank Fixtures...{Colors.ENDC}")
        if os.path.exists(os.path.join(self.project_dir, "load-fixtures.sh")):
            success = self.run_command("chmod +x load-fixtures.sh && ./load-fixtures.sh", "Loading fixtures")
            if success:
                print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Fixtures loaded successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
            else:
                print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Failed to load fixtures{Colors.ENDC}")
        else:
            print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ load-fixtures.sh not found{Colors.ENDC}")

    def health_check(self):
        """Check API health"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ¥ Checking PowerBank API Health...{Colors.ENDC}")
        
        # Check if containers are running
        status = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} ps --services --filter status=running", show_output=False)
        if status:
            running_services = status.split('\n') if status else []
            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}Running Services: {len(running_services)}{Colors.ENDC}")
            for service in running_services:
                print(f"  âœ… {service}")
        
        # Check API endpoint (try both with and without trailing slash)
        health_urls = [
            "http://localhost:8010/api/app/health",
            "http://localhost:8010/api/app/health/"
        ]
        
        health_passed = False
        for url in health_urls:
            health_check = self.run_command(f"curl -s -f {url}", show_output=False)
            if health_check:
                print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… API Health Check: PASSED{Colors.ENDC}")
                print(f"URL: {url}")
                print(f"Response: {health_check}")
                health_passed = True
                break
        
        if not health_passed:
            print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ API Health Check: FAILED{Colors.ENDC}")
            print("Tried both /api/app/health and /api/app/health/")

    def full_redeploy(self):
        """Full redeployment"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}ðŸ”„ Starting Full Redeployment...{Colors.ENDC}")
        confirm = input(f"{Colors.YELLOW}This will rebuild everything. Continue? (y/N): {Colors.ENDC}")
        
        if confirm.lower() == 'y':
            # Clear port conflicts before redeployment
            self.clear_port_conflicts()
            
            # Make sure script is executable
            self.run_command("chmod +x deploy-production.sh", show_output=False)
            
            success = self.run_command("./deploy-production.sh", "Running full deployment")
            if success:
                print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Full redeployment completed!{Colors.ENDC}")
                time.sleep(3)
                self.show_status()
            else:
                print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Redeployment failed{Colors.ENDC}")
        else:
            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}âŒ Redeployment cancelled{Colors.ENDC}")

    def database_backup(self):
        """Create database backup"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ’¾ Creating Database Backup...{Colors.ENDC}")
        timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
        backup_file = f"backups/powerbank_backup_{timestamp}.sql"
        
        # Ensure backups directory exists
        os.makedirs("backups", exist_ok=True)
        
        cmd = f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} exec -T db pg_dump -U powerbank_user powerbank_db > {backup_file}"
        success = self.run_command(cmd, f"Creating backup: {backup_file}")
        
        if success:
            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Backup created: {backup_file}{Colors.ENDC}")
        else:
            print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Backup failed{Colors.ENDC}")

    def clean_docker(self):
        """Clean unused Docker resources"""
        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ§¹ Cleaning Docker Resources...{Colors.ENDC}")
        commands = [
            "docker system prune -f",
            "docker volume prune -f",
            "docker image prune -f"
        ]
        
        for cmd in commands:
            self.run_command(cmd)
        
        print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Docker cleanup completed!{Colors.ENDC}")

    def run(self):
        """Main menu loop"""
        while True:
            try:
                os.system('clear' if os.name == 'posix' else 'cls')
                self.print_header()
                self.print_menu()
                
                choice = input(f"{Colors.BOLD}Enter your choice (0-11): {Colors.ENDC}").strip()
                
                if choice == "0":
                    print(f"\n{Colors.GREEN}ðŸ‘‹ Goodbye!{Colors.ENDC}")
                    break
                elif choice == "1":
                    self.restart_services()
                elif choice == "2":
                    self.stop_services()
                elif choice == "3":
                    self.delete_all()
                elif choice == "4":
                    self.show_status()
                elif choice == "5":
                    self.view_logs()
                elif choice == "6":
                    self.load_fixtures()
                elif choice == "7":
                    self.health_check()
                elif choice == "8":
                    self.full_redeploy()
                elif choice == "9":
                    self.database_backup()
                elif choice == "10":
                    self.clean_docker()
                elif choice == "11":
                    self.clear_port_conflicts()
                else:
                    print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Invalid choice. Please try again.{Colors.ENDC}")
                
                if choice != "0":
                    input(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Press Enter to continue...{Colors.ENDC}")
                    
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                print(f"\n\n{Colors.GREEN}ðŸ‘‹ Goodbye!{Colors.ENDC}")
                break
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ An error occurred: {e}{Colors.ENDC}")
                input(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Press Enter to continue...{Colors.ENDC}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Check if running as root
    if os.geteuid() != 0:
        print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ This script must be run as root{Colors.ENDC}")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    # Change to project directory
    project_dir = "/opt/powerbank"
    if os.path.exists(project_dir):
        os.chdir(project_dir)
    else:
        print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Project directory {project_dir} not found{Colors.ENDC}")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    manager = PowerBankManager()
    manager.run()

```

<summary>Tool output: call_wsQWsL8Kz8yXf30ERsIqIbEA</summary>

```text
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
#!/bin/bash

# PowerBank Django Production Deployment Script
# Clean deployment focused on containers and fixtures

set -e  # Exit on any error

echo "ðŸš€ PowerBank Django Production Deployment"
echo "=========================================="

# Configuration
PROJECT_DIR="/opt/User-Backend"
REPO_URL="https://github.com/ChargeGhar/User-Backend.git"
BRANCH="main"
DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE="docker-compose.prod.yml"

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

print_status() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[âœ“]${NC} $1"
}

print_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[âœ—]${NC} $1"
}

print_step() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}[STEP]${NC} $1"
}

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
   print_error "This script must be run as root"
   exit 1
fi

# Create and navigate to project directory
if [[ ! -d "$PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR"
fi
cd "$PROJECT_DIR"

# Update repository
print_step "Repository Management..."
if [[ -d ".git" ]]; then
    # Show current git status
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Current Git Status:${NC}"
    echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
    echo "Last commit: $(git log -1 --oneline)"
    echo ""
    
    # Show available branches
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Available branches:${NC}"
    git branch -a | grep -E "(main|master|develop|staging)" | head -5
    echo ""
    
    # Interactive menu for git operations
    echo -e "${BLUE}Git Update Options:${NC}"
    echo "1. ðŸ”„ Pull latest from current branch ($(git branch --show-current))"
    echo "2. ðŸ”€ Switch to main branch and pull"
    echo "3. ðŸ”€ Switch to different branch"
    echo "4. ðŸ—‘ï¸  Hard reset to remote (discards local changes)"
    echo "5. ðŸ’¾ Stash changes and pull"
    echo "6. â­ï¸  Skip git update (use current code)"
    echo ""
    
    read -p "Select option (1-6): " git_choice
    
    case $git_choice in
        1)
            print_step "Pulling latest from current branch..."
            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)"
            ;;
        2)
            print_step "Switching to main branch and pulling..."
            git stash push -m "Auto-stash before switching to main $(date)" || true
            git checkout "$BRANCH" || git checkout -b "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
            git pull origin "$BRANCH"
            ;;
        3)
            echo ""
            echo -e "${YELLOW}Available branches:${NC}"
            git branch -a | sed 's/remotes\/origin\///' | grep -v HEAD | sort | uniq | nl
            echo ""
            read -p "Enter branch name: " custom_branch
            if [[ -n "$custom_branch" ]]; then
                print_step "Switching to branch: $custom_branch"
                git stash push -m "Auto-stash before switching to $custom_branch $(date)" || true
                git checkout "$custom_branch" || git checkout -b "$custom_branch" "origin/$custom_branch"
                git pull origin "$custom_branch" || true
                BRANCH="$custom_branch"  # Update branch variable
            else
                print_error "No branch specified, using current branch"
            fi
            ;;
        4)
            print_step "Hard reset to remote (WARNING: This will discard local changes)..."
            read -p "Are you sure? Type 'YES' to confirm: " confirm
            if [[ "$confirm" == "YES" ]]; then
                git fetch origin
                git reset --hard "origin/$(git branch --show-current)"
                print_status "Hard reset completed"
            else
                print_status "Hard reset cancelled"
            fi
            ;;
        5)
            print_step "Stashing changes and pulling..."
            git stash push -m "Manual stash before deployment $(date)"
            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)"
            ;;
        6)
            print_status "Skipping git update, using current code"
            ;;
        *)
            print_error "Invalid choice, defaulting to pull current branch"
            git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)" || true
            ;;
    esac
    
    print_status "Repository updated"
else
    print_step "Cloning repository for first time..."
    git clone "$REPO_URL" .
    git checkout "$BRANCH"
    print_status "Repository cloned"
fi

# Configure environment for production
print_step "Configuring production environment..."
cp .env .env.backup 2>/dev/null || true
sed -i 's|ENVIRONMENT=local|ENVIRONMENT=production|' .env
sed -i 's|DJANGO_DEBUG=true|DJANGO_DEBUG=false|' .env
sed -i 's|CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=true|CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=false|' .env
sed -i 's|CELERY_TASK_EAGER_PROPAGATES=true|CELERY_TASK_EAGER_PROPAGATES=false|' .env
sed -i 's|CELERY_TASK_IGNORE_RESULT=true|CELERY_TASK_IGNORE_RESULT=false|' .env
# Update service names to match production docker-compose
sed -i 's|POSTGRES_HOST=pgbouncer|POSTGRES_HOST=db|' .env
sed -i 's|POSTGRES_HOST=.*|POSTGRES_HOST=db|' .env
sed -i 's|REDIS_HOST=.*|REDIS_HOST=redis|' .env
sed -i 's|RABBITMQ_HOST=.*|RABBITMQ_HOST=rabbitmq|' .env
sed -i 's|BASE_URL=http://localhost:8010|BASE_URL=https://main.chargeghar.com|' .env
# Update frontend URL for production redirects
sed -i 's|FRONTEND_URL=.*|FRONTEND_URL=https://chargeghar.app|' .env
# Update CORS and CSRF origins for production
sed -i 's|CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=.*|CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://main.chargeghar.com,https://chargeghar.app,https://app.chargeghar.com,http://localhost,http://213.210.21.113:8081|' .env
sed -i 's|CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS=.*|CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS=https://main.chargeghar.com,https://chargeghar.app,https://app.chargeghar.com,http://localhost,http://213.210.21.113:8081|' .env
print_status "Environment configured"

# Create directories
mkdir -p logs staticfiles backups

# Stop existing containers and clean up
print_step "Stopping containers and cleaning up..."
# IMPORTANT: Removed --volumes flag to preserve database data
docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" down --remove-orphans || true

# Also stop old containers from previous project name (one-time migration)
if docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}" | grep -q "^cg-"; then
    print_step "Removing old cg-* containers from previous project..."
    docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}" | grep "^cg-" | xargs -r docker rm -f || true
fi

# Kill any containers using port 8010
print_step "Checking for port conflicts..."
API_PORT=$(grep "API_PORT" .env | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d ' ')
PORT_TO_CHECK=${API_PORT:-8010}

# Find and stop containers using the port
CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS=$(docker ps --filter "publish=$PORT_TO_CHECK" --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null || true)
if [[ -n "$CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS" ]]; then
    print_step "Found containers using port $PORT_TO_CHECK, stopping them..."
    echo "$CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS" | while read container; do
        echo "Stopping container: $container"
        docker stop "$container" || true
        docker rm "$container" || true
    done
fi

# Also check for any remaining processes on the port
PROCESS_ON_PORT=$(netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ":$PORT_TO_CHECK " | awk '{print $7}' | cut -d'/' -f1 || true)
if [[ -n "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" && "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" != "-" ]]; then
    print_step "Killing process $PROCESS_ON_PORT using port $PORT_TO_CHECK..."
    kill -9 "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

docker system prune -f
print_status "Cleanup completed"

# Build and start services
print_step "Building and starting services..."
docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" build --no-cache
docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" up -d
print_status "Services started"

# Wait for services to be ready
print_step "Waiting for services to initialize..."
sleep 30

# Check migration status
print_step "Checking migration status..."
if docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations | grep -q "Exit 0"; then
    print_status "Migrations completed successfully"
elif docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations | grep -q "Exit"; then
    print_error "Migrations failed! Checking logs..."
    docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" logs migrations
    exit 1
else
    print_step "Migrations still running, waiting..."
    sleep 30
fi

# Ensure static files are collected
print_step "Collecting static files..."
docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T api python manage.py collectstatic --noinput || true

# Show container status
print_step "Container Status:"
docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps

# Check for any failed services (excluding successful one-time services)
print_step "Checking for failed services..."
FAILED_SERVICES=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps --filter "status=exited" --format "table {{.Service}}\t{{.Status}}" | grep -v "SERVICE" | grep -v "Exit 0" | grep -v "migrations" | grep -v "collectstatic" || true)
if [[ -n "$FAILED_SERVICES" ]]; then
    print_error "Some services failed:"
    echo "$FAILED_SERVICES"
    print_step "Showing logs for failed services..."
    docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" logs --tail=50
    exit 1
else
    print_status "All services are running successfully!"
    
    # Check one-time services completed successfully
    MIGRATION_STATUS=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps migrations --format "{{.Status}}" | grep "Exit 0" || echo "")
    COLLECTSTATIC_STATUS=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" ps collectstatic --format "{{.Status}}" | grep "Exit 0" || echo "")
    
    if [[ -n "$MIGRATION_STATUS" ]]; then
        print_status "âœ… Database migrations completed successfully"
    fi
    
    if [[ -n "$COLLECTSTATIC_STATUS" ]]; then
        print_status "âœ… Static files collection completed successfully"
    fi
fi

# Auto-load fixtures (smart selection based on deployment state)
print_step "Loading fixtures..."
if [[ -f "load-fixtures-safe.sh" ]] && [[ -f "load-fixtures.sh" ]]; then
    # Check if database already has data (indicating existing deployment)
    DB_HAS_DATA=$(docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T api python manage.py shell -c "
from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
from api.stations.models import Station
User = get_user_model()
user_count = User.objects.count()
station_count = Station.objects.count()
if user_count > 0 or station_count > 0:
    print('EXISTING')
else:
    print('NEW')
" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r' | tail -1)

    if [[ "$DB_HAS_DATA" == "EXISTING" ]]; then
        print_status "Existing deployment detected, using safe fixtures loader..."
        chmod +x load-fixtures-safe.sh
        ./load-fixtures-safe.sh
        print_status "Fixtures loaded safely (existing deployment)"
    else
        print_status "New deployment detected, using full fixtures loader..."
        chmod +x load-fixtures.sh
        ./load-fixtures.sh
        print_status "Fixtures loaded (new deployment)"
    fi
elif [[ -f "load-fixtures-safe.sh" ]]; then
    print_warning "Only safe fixtures loader available, using it..."
    chmod +x load-fixtures-safe.sh
    ./load-fixtures-safe.sh
    print_status "Fixtures loaded safely"
elif [[ -f "load-fixtures.sh" ]]; then
    print_warning "Only regular fixtures loader available, using it..."
    chmod +x load-fixtures.sh
    ./load-fixtures.sh
    print_status "Fixtures loaded"
else
    print_error "No fixtures loader found, skipping fixture loading"
fi

# Final status
API_PORT=$(grep "API_PORT" .env | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d ' ')
SERVER_IP=$(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}')

print_step ""
print_status "ðŸŽ‰ ChargeGhar Deployment Completed!"
print_status "========================================"
print_status "API URL: https://main.chargeghar.com"
print_status "API Documentation: https://main.chargeghar.com/docs/"
print_status "Admin Panel: https://main.chargeghar.com/admin/"
print_status ""
print_status "Use 'python3 mgr.py' for management tasks"
print_status ""

```

```text
  12:

  15:     BLUE = '\033[94m'
  16:     CYAN = '\033[96m'
  17:     GREEN = '\033[92m'
  18:     YELLOW = '\033[93m'

  25:     def __init__(self):
  26:         self.compose_file = "docker-compose.prod.yml"
  27:         self.project_dir = "/opt/powerbank"
  28:         
  29:     def print_header(self):
  30:         print(f"\n{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}")
  31:         print(f"{Colors.BOLD}{Colors.HEADER}ðŸš€ PowerBank Django Deployment Manager{Colors.ENDC}")
  32:         print(f"{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}")
  33:         print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Current Time: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}{Colors.ENDC}")
  34:         print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Project Directory: {self.project_dir}{Colors.ENDC}")
  35:         print(f"{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}\n")
  36: 
  37:     def print_menu(self):
  38:         menu_items = [
  39:             ("1", "ðŸ”„ Restart Services", "Restart all PowerBank containers"),
  40:             ("2", "â¹ï¸  Stop Services", "Stop all PowerBank containers"),
  41:             ("3", "ðŸ—‘ï¸  Delete All", "Stop and remove all containers, volumes, images"),
  42:             ("4", "ðŸ“Š Service Status", "Show current status of all services"),
  43:             ("5", "ðŸ“‹ View Logs", "View logs for all services or specific service"),
  44:             ("6", "ðŸ”§ Load Fixtures", "Load sample data into the database"),
  45:             ("7", "ðŸ¥ Health Check", "Check API health and connectivity"),
  46:             ("8", "ðŸ”„ Full Redeploy", "Complete redeployment (git pull + rebuild)"),
  47:             ("9", "ðŸ’¾ Database Backup", "Create database backup"),
  48:             ("10", "ðŸ§¹ Clean Docker", "Clean unused Docker resources"),
  49:             ("11", "ðŸ”“ Clear Port Conflicts", "Fix port conflicts (8010)"),
  50:             ("0", "âŒ Exit", "Exit the manager")
  51:         ]
  52:         
  53:         print(f"{Colors.BOLD}{Colors.BLUE}Available Actions:{Colors.ENDC}\n")
  54:         for num, title, desc in menu_items:
  55:             print(f"{Colors.GREEN}{num:>2}.{Colors.ENDC} {Colors.BOLD}{title:<20}{Colors.ENDC} - {desc}")
  56:         print()
  57: 
  58:     def run_command(self, command, description="", show_output=True):
  59:         """Run a shell command with nice formatting"""
  60:         if description:
  61:             print(f"{Colors.BLUE}[RUNNING]{Colors.ENDC} {description}...")
  62:         
  63:         try:
  64:             if show_output:
  65:                 result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True, cwd=self.project_dir)
  66:                 return result.returncode == 0
  67:             else:
  68:                 result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=self.project_dir)
  69:                 return result.stdout.strip()
  70:         except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
  71:             print(f"{Colors.RED}[ERROR]{Colors.ENDC} Command failed: {e}")
  72:             return False
  73: 
  74:     def clear_port_conflicts(self):
  75:         """Clear any port conflicts before deployment"""
  76:         print(f"{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ” Checking for port conflicts...{Colors.ENDC}")
  77:         
  78:         # Get API port from .env
  79:         try:
  80:             with open('.env', 'r') as f:
  81:                 for line in f:
  82:                     if line.startswith('API_PORT='):
  83:                         port = line.split('=')[1].strip()
  84:                         break
  85:                 else:
  86:                     port = "8010"  # default
  87:         except:
  88:             port = "8010"
  89:         
  90:         # Find containers using the port
  91:         conflicting = self.run_command(f'docker ps --filter "publish={port}" --format "{{{{.Names}}}}"', show_output=False)
  92:         if conflicting:
  93:             print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Found containers using port {port}:{Colors.ENDC}")
  94:             for container in conflicting.split('\n'):
  95:                 if container.strip():
  96:                     print(f"  Stopping: {container}")
  97:                     self.run_command(f"docker stop {container}", show_output=False)
  98:                     self.run_command(f"docker rm {container}", show_output=False)
  99:         
 100:         # Check for processes on port
 101:         process_check = self.run_command(f'netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ":{port} "', show_output=False)
 102:         if process_check:
 103:             print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Port {port} is still in use, attempting to free it...{Colors.ENDC}")
 104:             # Kill docker-proxy processes if any
 105:             self.run_command("pkill -f docker-proxy", show_output=False)
 106:         
 107:         print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Port conflicts cleared{Colors.ENDC}")
 108: 
 109:     def restart_services(self):
 110:         """Restart all services"""
 111:         print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}ðŸ”„ Restarting PowerBank Services...{Colors.ENDC}")
 112:         
 113:         # Clear port conflicts first
 114:         self.clear_port_conflicts()
 115:         
 116:         # Stop and start instead of restart to avoid port issues
 117:         self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down", "Stopping containers")
 118:         time.sleep(3)
 119:         success = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} up -d", "Starting containers")
 120:         
 121:         if success:
 122:             print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Services restarted successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
 123:             time.sleep(5)
 124:             self.show_status()
 125:         else:
 126:             print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Failed to restart services{Colors.ENDC}")
 127: 
 128:     def stop_services(self):
 129:         """Stop all services"""
 130:         print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}â¹ï¸ Stopping PowerBank Services...{Colors.ENDC}")
 131:         
 132:         # Clear port conflicts to ensure clean shutdown
 133:         self.clear_port_conflicts()
 134:         
 135:         success = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down --remove-orphans", "Stopping containers")
 136:         if success:
 137:             print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Services stopped successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
 138:         else:
 139:             print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Failed to stop services{Colors.ENDC}")
 140: 
 141:     def delete_all(self):
 142:         """Delete all containers, volumes, and images"""
 143:         print(f"\n{Colors.RED}ðŸ—‘ï¸ WARNING: This will delete ALL PowerBank data!{Colors.ENDC}")
 144:         confirm = input(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Type 'DELETE' to confirm: {Colors.ENDC}")
 145:         
 146:         if confirm == "DELETE":
 147:             print(f"{Colors.RED}Deleting all PowerBank resources...{Colors.ENDC}")
 148:             commands = [
 149:                 f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down --remove-orphans --volumes",
 150:                 "docker system prune -af",
 151:                 "docker volume prune -f"
 152:             ]
 153:             
 154:             for cmd in commands:
 155:                 self.run_command(cmd)
 156:             
 157:             print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… All resources deleted!{Colors.ENDC}")
 158:         else:
 159:             print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}âŒ Deletion cancelled{Colors.ENDC}")
 160: 
 161:     def show_status(self):
 162:         """Show service status"""
 163:         print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ“Š PowerBank Service Status:{Colors.ENDC}")
 164:         self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} ps")
 165: 
 166:     def view_logs(self):
 167:         """View logs with service selection"""
 168:         print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ“‹ Available Services:{Colors.ENDC}")
 169:         services = ["all", "api", "db", "redis", "rabbitmq", "celery"]
 170:         
 171:         for i, service in enumerate(services, 1):
 172:             print(f"{Colors.GREEN}{i}.{Colors.ENDC} {service}")
 173:         
 174:         try:
 175:             choice = int(input(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Select service (1-{len(services)}): {Colors.ENDC}"))
 176:             if 1 <= choice <= len(services):
 177:                 service = services[choice - 1]
 178:                 if service == "all":
 179:                     cmd = f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} logs --tail=50 -f"
 180:                 else:
 181:                     cmd = f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} logs --tail=50 -f {service}"
 182:                 
 183:                 print(f"{Colors.BLUE}Showing logs for {service} (Press Ctrl+C to exit)...{Colors.ENDC}")
 184:                 self.run_command(cmd)
 185:             else:
 186:                 print(f"{Colors.RED}Invalid choice{Colors.ENDC}")
 187:         except (ValueError, KeyboardInterrupt):
 188:             print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Returning to menu...{Colors.ENDC}")
 189: 
 190:     def load_fixtures(self):
 191:         """Load fixtures"""
 192:         print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ”§ Loading PowerBank Fixtures...{Colors.ENDC}")
 193:         if os.path.exists(os.path.join(self.project_dir, "load-fixtures.sh")):
 194:             success = self.run_command("chmod +x load-fixtures.sh && ./load-fixtures.sh", "Loading fixtures")
 195:             if success:
 196:                 print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Fixtures loaded successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
 197:             else:
 198:                 print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Failed to load fixtures{Colors.ENDC}")
 199:         else:
 200:             print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ load-fixtures.sh not found{Colors.ENDC}")
 201: 
 202:     def health_check(self):
 203:         """Check API health"""
 204:         print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ¥ Checking PowerBank API Health...{Colors.ENDC}")
 205:         
 206:         # Check if containers are running
 207:         status = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} ps --services --filter status=running", show_output=False)
 208:         if status:
 209:             running_services = status.split('\n') if status else []
 210:             print(f"{Colors.GREEN}Running Services: {len(running_services)}{Colors.ENDC}")
 211:             for service in running_services:
 212:                 print(f"  âœ… {service}")
 213:         
 214:         # Check API endpoint (try both with and without trailing slash)
 215:         health_urls = [
 216:             "http://localhost:8010/api/app/health",
 217:             "http://localhost:8010/api/app/health/"
 218:         ]
 219:         
 220:         health_passed = False
 221:         for url in health_urls:
 222:             health_check = self.run_command(f"curl -s -f {url}", show_output=False)
 223:             if health_check:
 224:                 print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… API Health Check: PASSED{Colors.ENDC}")
 225:                 print(f"URL: {url}")
 226:                 print(f"Response: {health_check}")
 227:                 health_passed = True
 228:                 break
 229:         
 230:         if not health_passed:
 231:             print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ API Health Check: FAILED{Colors.ENDC}")
 232:             print("Tried both /api/app/health and /api/app/health/")
 233: 
 234:     def full_redeploy(self):
 235:         """Full redeployment"""
 236:         print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}ðŸ”„ Starting Full Redeployment...{Colors.ENDC}")
 237:         confirm = input(f"{Colors.YELLOW}This will rebuild everything. Continue? (y/N): {Colors.ENDC}")
 238:         
 239:         if confirm.lower() == 'y':
 240:             # Clear port conflicts before redeployment
 241:             self.clear_port_conflicts()
 242:             
 243:             # Make sure script is executable
 244:             self.run_command("chmod +x deploy-production.sh", show_output=False)
 245:             
 246:             success = self.run_command("./deploy-production.sh", "Running full deployment")
 247:             if success:
 248:                 print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Full redeployment completed!{Colors.ENDC}")
 249:                 time.sleep(3)
 250:                 self.show_status()
 251:             else:
 252:                 print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Redeployment failed{Colors.ENDC}")
 253:         else:
 254:             print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}âŒ Redeployment cancelled{Colors.ENDC}")
 255: 
 256:     def database_backup(self):
 257:         """Create database backup"""
 258:         print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ’¾ Creating Database Backup...{Colors.ENDC}")
 259:         timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
 260:         backup_file = f"backups/powerbank_backup_{timestamp}.sql"
 261:         
 262:         # Ensure backups directory exists
 263:         os.makedirs("backups", exist_ok=True)
 264:         
 265:         cmd = f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} exec -T db pg_dump -U powerbank_user powerbank_db > {backup_file}"
 266:         success = self.run_command(cmd, f"Creating backup: {backup_file}")
 267:         
 268:         if success:
 269:             print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Backup created: {backup_file}{Colors.ENDC}")
 270:         else:
 271:             print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Backup failed{Colors.ENDC}")
 272: 
 273:     def clean_docker(self):
 274:         """Clean unused Docker resources"""
 275:         print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}ðŸ§¹ Cleaning Docker Resources...{Colors.ENDC}")
 276:         commands = [
 277:             "docker system prune -f",
 278:             "docker volume prune -f",
 279:             "docker image prune -f"
 280:         ]
 281:         
 282:         for cmd in commands:
 283:             self.run_command(cmd)
 284:         
 285:         print(f"{Colors.GREEN}âœ… Docker cleanup completed!{Colors.ENDC}")
 286: 
 287:     def run(self):
 288:         """Main menu loop"""
 289:         while True:
 290:             try:
 291:                 os.system('clear' if os.name == 'posix' else 'cls')
 292:                 self.print_header()
 293:                 self.print_menu()
 294:                 
 295:                 choice = input(f"{Colors.BOLD}Enter your choice (0-11): {Colors.ENDC}").strip()
 296:                 
 297:                 if choice == "0":
 298:                     print(f"\n{Colors.GREEN}ðŸ‘‹ Goodbye!{Colors.ENDC}")
 299:                     break
 300:                 elif choice == "1":
 301:                     self.restart_services()
 302:                 elif choice == "2":
 303:                     self.stop_services()
 304:                 elif choice == "3":
 305:                     self.delete_all()
 306:                 elif choice == "4":
 307:                     self.show_status()
 308:                 elif choice == "5":
 309:                     self.view_logs()
 310:                 elif choice == "6":
 311:                     self.load_fixtures()
 312:                 elif choice == "7":
 313:                     self.health_check()
 314:                 elif choice == "8":
 315:                     self.full_redeploy()
 316:                 elif choice == "9":
 317:                     self.database_backup()
 318:                 elif choice == "10":
 319:                     self.clean_docker()
 320:                 elif choice == "11":
 321:                     self.clear_port_conflicts()
 322:                 else:
 323:                     print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Invalid choice. Please try again.{Colors.ENDC}")
 324:                 
 325:                 if choice != "0":
 326:                     input(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Press Enter to continue...{Colors.ENDC}")
 327:                     
 328:             except KeyboardInterrupt:
 329:                 print(f"\n\n{Colors.GREEN}ðŸ‘‹ Goodbye!{Colors.ENDC}")
 330:                 break
 331:             except Exception as e:
 332:                 print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ An error occurred: {e}{Colors.ENDC}")
 333:                 input(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Press Enter to continue...{Colors.ENDC}")
 334: 
 335: if __name__ == "__main__":
 336:     # Check if running as root
 337:     if os.geteuid() != 0:
 338:         print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ This script must be run as root{Colors.ENDC}")
 339:         sys.exit(1)
 340:     
 341:     # Change to project directory
 342:     project_dir = "/opt/powerbank"
 343:     if os.path.exists(project_dir):
 344:         os.chdir(project_dir)
 345:     else:
 346:         print(f"{Colors.RED}âŒ Project directory {project_dir} not found{Colors.ENDC}")
 347:         sys.exit(1)
 348:     
 349:     manager = PowerBankManager()
 350:     manager.run()
```

```text
  37: if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  38:    print_error "This script must be run as root"
  39:    exit 1
  40: fi
  41: 
  42: # Create and navigate to project directory
  43: if [[ ! -d "$PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
  44:     mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR"
  45: fi

```

```text
   1: #!/bin/bash
   2: 
   3: # PowerBank Django Production Deployment Script
   4: # Clean deployment focused on containers and fixtures
   5: 
   6: set -e  # Exit on any error
   7: 
   8: echo "ðŸš€ PowerBank Django Production Deployment"
   9: echo "=========================================="
  10: 
  11: # Configuration
  12: PROJECT_DIR="${PROJECT_DIR:-/opt/User-Backend}"
  13: if [[ ! -d "$PROJECT_DIR" ]] && [[ -d "/opt/powerbank" ]]; then
  14:     PROJECT_DIR="/opt/powerbank"
  15: fi
  16: REPO_URL="https://github.com/ChargeGhar/User-Backend.git"
  17: BRANCH="main"
  18: DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE="docker-compose.prod.yml"
  19: ENV_PROD_FILE="${ENV_PROD_FILE:-.env.prod}"
  20: 
  21: # Non-interactive controls (used by mgr.py full redeploy)
  22: DEPLOY_NON_INTERACTIVE="${DEPLOY_NON_INTERACTIVE:-0}"
  23: DEPLOY_GIT_CHOICE="${DEPLOY_GIT_CHOICE:-1}"
  24: DEPLOY_TARGET_BRANCH="${DEPLOY_TARGET_BRANCH:-}"
  25: DEPLOY_CONFIRM_RESET="${DEPLOY_CONFIRM_RESET:-NO}"
  26: 
  27: # Colors for output
  28: RED='\033[0;31m'
  29: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
  30: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
  31: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
  32: NC='\033[0m' # No Color
  33: 
  34: print_status() {
  35:     echo -e "${GREEN}[âœ“]${NC} $1"
  36: }
  37: 
  38: print_error() {
  39:     echo -e "${RED}[âœ—]${NC} $1"
  40: }
  41: 
  42: print_warning() {
  43:     echo -e "${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $1"
  44: }
  45: 
  46: print_step() {
  47:     echo -e "${BLUE}[STEP]${NC} $1"
  48: }
  49: 
  50: # Check if running as root
  51: if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  52:    print_error "This script must be run as root"
  53:    exit 1
  54: fi
  55: 
  56: # Create and navigate to project directory
  57: if [[ ! -d "$PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
  58:     mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR"
  59: fi
  60: cd "$PROJECT_DIR"
  61: 
  62: # Update repository
  63: print_step "Repository Management..."
  64: if [[ -d ".git" ]]; then
  65:     # Show current git status
  66:     echo ""
  67:     echo -e "${YELLOW}Current Git Status:${NC}"
  68:     echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
  69:     echo "Last commit: $(git log -1 --oneline)"
  70:     echo ""
  71:     
  72:     # Show available branches
  73:     echo -e "${YELLOW}Available branches:${NC}"
  74:     git branch -a | grep -E "(main|master|develop|staging)" | head -5
  75:     echo ""
  76:     
  77:     # Interactive menu for git operations
  78:     echo -e "${BLUE}Git Update Options:${NC}"
  79:     echo "1. ðŸ”„ Pull latest from current branch ($(git branch --show-current))"
  80:     echo "2. ðŸ”€ Switch to main branch and pull"
  81:     echo "3. ðŸ”€ Switch to different branch"
  82:     echo "4. ðŸ—‘ï¸  Hard reset to remote (discards local changes)"
  83:     echo "5. ðŸ’¾ Stash changes and pull"
  84:     echo "6. â­ï¸  Skip git update (use current code)"
  85:     echo ""
  86:     
  87:     if [[ "$DEPLOY_NON_INTERACTIVE" == "1" ]]; then
  88:         git_choice="$DEPLOY_GIT_CHOICE"
  89:         echo "Select option (1-6): $git_choice (non-interactive)"
  90:     else
  91:         read -p "Select option (1-6): " git_choice
  92:     fi
  93:     
  94:     case $git_choice in
  95:         1)
  96:             print_step "Pulling latest from current branch..."
  97:             git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)"
  98:             ;;
  99:         2)
 100:             print_step "Switching to main branch and pulling..."
 101:             git stash push -m "Auto-stash before switching to main $(date)" || true
 102:             git checkout "$BRANCH" || git checkout -b "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
 103:             git pull origin "$BRANCH"
 104:             ;;
 105:         3)
 106:             echo ""
 107:             echo -e "${YELLOW}Available branches:${NC}"
 108:             git branch -a | sed 's/remotes\/origin\///' | grep -v HEAD | sort | uniq | nl
 109:             echo ""
 110:             if [[ "$DEPLOY_NON_INTERACTIVE" == "1" ]]; then
 111:                 custom_branch="$DEPLOY_TARGET_BRANCH"
 112:                 echo "Enter branch name: $custom_branch (non-interactive)"
 113:             else
 114:                 read -p "Enter branch name: " custom_branch
 115:             fi
 116:             if [[ -n "$custom_branch" ]]; then
 117:                 print_step "Switching to branch: $custom_branch"
 118:                 git stash push -m "Auto-stash before switching to $custom_branch $(date)" || true
 119:                 git checkout "$custom_branch" || git checkout -b "$custom_branch" "origin/$custom_branch"
 120:                 git pull origin "$custom_branch" || true
 121:                 BRANCH="$custom_branch"  # Update branch variable
 122:             else
 123:                 print_error "No branch specified, using current branch"
 124:             fi
 125:             ;;
 126:         4)
 127:             print_step "Hard reset to remote (WARNING: This will discard local changes)..."
 128:             if [[ "$DEPLOY_NON_INTERACTIVE" == "1" ]]; then
 129:                 confirm="$DEPLOY_CONFIRM_RESET"
 130:                 echo "Are you sure? Type 'YES' to confirm: $confirm (non-interactive)"
 131:             else
 132:                 read -p "Are you sure? Type 'YES' to confirm: " confirm
 133:             fi
 134:             if [[ "$confirm" == "YES" ]]; then
 135:                 git fetch origin
 136:                 git reset --hard "origin/$(git branch --show-current)"
 137:                 print_status "Hard reset completed"
 138:             else
 139:                 print_status "Hard reset cancelled"
 140:             fi
 141:             ;;
 142:         5)
 143:             print_step "Stashing changes and pulling..."
 144:             git stash push -m "Manual stash before deployment $(date)"
 145:             git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)"
 146:             ;;
 147:         6)
 148:             print_status "Skipping git update, using current code"
 149:             ;;
 150:         *)
 151:             print_error "Invalid choice, defaulting to pull current branch"
 152:             git pull origin "$(git branch --show-current)" || true
 153:             ;;
 154:     esac
 155:     
 156:     print_status "Repository updated"
 157: else
 158:     print_step "Cloning repository for first time..."
 159:     git clone "$REPO_URL" .
 160:     git checkout "$BRANCH"
 161:     print_status "Repository cloned"
 162: fi
 163: 
 164: # Configure environment for production
 165: print_step "Configuring production environment..."
 166: cp .env .env.backup 2>/dev/null || true
 167: if [[ ! -f "$ENV_PROD_FILE" ]]; then
 168:     print_error "$ENV_PROD_FILE not found in $PROJECT_DIR"
 169:     print_error "Create $ENV_PROD_FILE first, then re-run deployment"
 170:     exit 1
 171: fi
 172: cp "$ENV_PROD_FILE" .env
 173: 
 174: # Keep internal service endpoints aligned with docker-compose networking.
 175: sed -i 's|^POSTGRES_HOST=.*|POSTGRES_HOST=db|' .env
 176: sed -i 's|^REDIS_HOST=.*|REDIS_HOST=redis|' .env
 177: sed -i 's|^REDIS_PORT=.*|REDIS_PORT=6379|' .env
 178: sed -i 's|^RABBITMQ_HOST=.*|RABBITMQ_HOST=rabbitmq|' .env
 179: print_status "Environment configured"
 180: 
 181: # Create directories
 182: mkdir -p logs staticfiles backups
 183: 
 184: # Stop existing containers and clean up
 185: print_step "Stopping containers and cleaning up..."
 186: # IMPORTANT: Removed --volumes flag to preserve database data
 187: docker-compose -f "$DOCKER_COMPOSE_FILE" down --remove-orphans || true
 188: 
 189: # Also stop old containers from previous project name (one-time migration)
 190: if docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}" | grep -q "^cg-"; then
 191:     print_step "Removing old cg-* containers from previous project..."
 192:     docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}" | grep "^cg-" | xargs -r docker rm -f || true
 193: fi
 194: 
 195: # Kill any containers using port 8010
 196: print_step "Checking for port conflicts..."
 197: API_PORT=$(grep "API_PORT" .env | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d ' ')
 198: PORT_TO_CHECK=${API_PORT:-8010}
 199: 
 200: # Find and stop containers using the port
 201: CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS=$(docker ps --filter "publish=$PORT_TO_CHECK" --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null || true)
 202: if [[ -n "$CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS" ]]; then
 203:     print_step "Found containers using port $PORT_TO_CHECK, stopping them..."
 204:     echo "$CONFLICTING_CONTAINERS" | while read container; do
 205:         echo "Stopping container: $container"
 206:         docker stop "$container" || true
 207:         docker rm "$container" || true
 208:     done
 209: fi
 210: 
 211: # Also check for any remaining processes on the port
 212: PROCESS_ON_PORT=$(netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ":$PORT_TO_CHECK " | awk '{print $7}' | cut -d'/' -f1 || true)
 213: if [[ -n "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" && "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" != "-" ]]; then
 214:     print_step "Killing process $PROCESS_ON_PORT using port $PORT_TO_CHECK..."
 215:     kill -9 "$PROCESS_ON_PORT" 2>/dev/null || true
 216: fi
 217: 
 218: docker system prune -f
 219: print_status "Cleanup completed"
 220: 

```

```text
-#!/usr/bin/env python3
-"""
-PowerBank Django Deployment Manager
-Beautiful menu-driven management tool for PowerBank Django deployment
-"""
-
-import os
-import sys
-import subprocess
-import time
-from datetime import datetime
-
-class Colors:
-    HEADER = '\033[95m'
-    BLUE = '\033[94m'
-    CYAN = '\033[96m'
-    GREEN = '\033[92m'
-    YELLOW = '\033[93m'
-    RED = '\033[91m'
-    ENDC = '\033[0m'
-    BOLD = '\033[1m'
-    UNDERLINE = '\033[4m'
-
-class PowerBankManager:
-    def __init__(self):
-        self.compose_file = "docker-compose.prod.yml"
-        self.project_dir = "/opt/powerbank"
-        
-    def print_header(self):
-        print(f"\n{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}")
-        print(f"{Colors.BOLD}{Colors.HEADER}🚀 PowerBank Django Deployment Manager{Colors.ENDC}")
-        print(f"{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}")
-        print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Current Time: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}{Colors.ENDC}")
-        print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Project Directory: {self.project_dir}{Colors.ENDC}")
-        print(f"{Colors.CYAN}{'='*60}{Colors.ENDC}\n")
-
-    def print_menu(self):
-        menu_items = [
-            ("1", "🔄 Restart Services", "Restart all PowerBank containers"),
-            ("2", "⏹️  Stop Services", "Stop all PowerBank containers"),
-            ("3", "🗑️  Delete All", "Stop and remove all containers, volumes, images"),
-            ("4", "📊 Service Status", "Show current status of all services"),
-            ("5", "📋 View Logs", "View logs for all services or specific service"),
-            ("6", "🔧 Load Fixtures", "Load sample data into the database"),
-            ("7", "🏥 Health Check", "Check API health and connectivity"),
-            ("8", "🔄 Full Redeploy", "Complete redeployment (git pull + rebuild)"),
-            ("9", "💾 Database Backup", "Create database backup"),
-            ("10", "🧹 Clean Docker", "Clean unused Docker resources"),
-            ("11", "🔓 Clear Port Conflicts", "Fix port conflicts (8010)"),
-            ("0", "❌ Exit", "Exit the manager")
-        ]
-        
-        print(f"{Colors.BOLD}{Colors.BLUE}Available Actions:{Colors.ENDC}\n")
-        for num, title, desc in menu_items:
-            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}{num:>2}.{Colors.ENDC} {Colors.BOLD}{title:<20}{Colors.ENDC} - {desc}")
-        print()
-
-    def run_command(self, command, description="", show_output=True):
-        """Run a shell command with nice formatting"""
-        if description:
-            print(f"{Colors.BLUE}[RUNNING]{Colors.ENDC} {description}...")
-        
-        try:
-            if show_output:
-                result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True, cwd=self.project_dir)
-                return result.returncode == 0
-            else:
-                result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=self.project_dir)
-                return result.stdout.strip()
-        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
-            print(f"{Colors.RED}[ERROR]{Colors.ENDC} Command failed: {e}")
-            return False
-
-    def clear_port_conflicts(self):
-        """Clear any port conflicts before deployment"""
-        print(f"{Colors.BLUE}🔍 Checking for port conflicts...{Colors.ENDC}")
-        
-        # Get API port from .env
-        try:
-            with open('.env', 'r') as f:
-                for line in f:
-                    if line.startswith('API_PORT='):
-                        port = line.split('=')[1].strip()
-                        break
-                else:
-                    port = "8010"  # default
-        except:
-            port = "8010"
-        
-        # Find containers using the port
-        conflicting = self.run_command(f'docker ps --filter "publish={port}" --format "{{{{.Names}}}}"', show_output=False)
-        if conflicting:
-            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Found containers using port {port}:{Colors.ENDC}")
-            for container in conflicting.split('\n'):
-                if container.strip():
-                    print(f"  Stopping: {container}")
-                    self.run_command(f"docker stop {container}", show_output=False)
-                    self.run_command(f"docker rm {container}", show_output=False)
-        
-        # Check for processes on port
-        process_check = self.run_command(f'netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ":{port} "', show_output=False)
-        if process_check:
-            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Port {port} is still in use, attempting to free it...{Colors.ENDC}")
-            # Kill docker-proxy processes if any
-            self.run_command("pkill -f docker-proxy", show_output=False)
-        
-        print(f"{Colors.GREEN}✅ Port conflicts cleared{Colors.ENDC}")
-
-    def restart_services(self):
-        """Restart all services"""
-        print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}🔄 Restarting PowerBank Services...{Colors.ENDC}")
-        
-        # Clear port conflicts first
-        self.clear_port_conflicts()
-        
-        # Stop and start instead of restart to avoid port issues
-        self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down", "Stopping containers")
-        time.sleep(3)
-        success = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} up -d", "Starting containers")
-        
-        if success:
-            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}✅ Services restarted successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
-            time.sleep(5)
-            self.show_status()
-        else:
-            print(f"{Colors.RED}❌ Failed to restart services{Colors.ENDC}")
-
-    def stop_services(self):
-        """Stop all services"""
-        print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}⏹️ Stopping PowerBank Services...{Colors.ENDC}")
-        
-        # Clear port conflicts to ensure clean shutdown
-        self.clear_port_conflicts()
-        
-        success = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down --remove-orphans", "Stopping containers")
-        if success:
-            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}✅ Services stopped successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
-        else:
-            print(f"{Colors.RED}❌ Failed to stop services{Colors.ENDC}")
-
-    def delete_all(self):
-        """Delete all containers, volumes, and images"""
-        print(f"\n{Colors.RED}🗑️ WARNING: This will delete ALL PowerBank data!{Colors.ENDC}")
-        confirm = input(f"{Colors.YELLOW}Type 'DELETE' to confirm: {Colors.ENDC}")
-        
-        if confirm == "DELETE":
-            print(f"{Colors.RED}Deleting all PowerBank resources...{Colors.ENDC}")
-            commands = [
-                f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} down --remove-orphans --volumes",
-                "docker system prune -af",
-                "docker volume prune -f"
-            ]
-            
-            for cmd in commands:
-                self.run_command(cmd)
-            
-            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}✅ All resources deleted!{Colors.ENDC}")
-        else:
-            print(f"{Colors.YELLOW}❌ Deletion cancelled{Colors.ENDC}")
-
-    def show_status(self):
-        """Show service status"""
-        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}📊 PowerBank Service Status:{Colors.ENDC}")
-        self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} ps")
-
-    def view_logs(self):
-        """View logs with service selection"""
-        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}📋 Available Services:{Colors.ENDC}")
-        services = ["all", "api", "db", "redis", "rabbitmq", "celery"]
-        
-        for i, service in enumerate(services, 1):
-            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}{i}.{Colors.ENDC} {service}")
-        
-        try:
-            choice = int(input(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Select service (1-{len(services)}): {Colors.ENDC}"))
-            if 1 <= choice <= len(services):
-                service = services[choice - 1]
-                if service == "all":
-                    cmd = f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} logs --tail=50 -f"
-                else:
-                    cmd = f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} logs --tail=50 -f {service}"
-                
-                print(f"{Colors.BLUE}Showing logs for {service} (Press Ctrl+C to exit)...{Colors.ENDC}")
-                self.run_command(cmd)
-            else:
-                print(f"{Colors.RED}Invalid choice{Colors.ENDC}")
-        except (ValueError, KeyboardInterrupt):
-            print(f"\n{Colors.YELLOW}Returning to menu...{Colors.ENDC}")
-
-    def load_fixtures(self):
-        """Load fixtures"""
-        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}🔧 Loading PowerBank Fixtures...{Colors.ENDC}")
-        if os.path.exists(os.path.join(self.project_dir, "load-fixtures.sh")):
-            success = self.run_command("chmod +x load-fixtures.sh && ./load-fixtures.sh", "Loading fixtures")
-            if success:
-                print(f"{Colors.GREEN}✅ Fixtures loaded successfully!{Colors.ENDC}")
-            else:
-                print(f"{Colors.RED}❌ Failed to load fixtures{Colors.ENDC}")
-        else:
-            print(f"{Colors.RED}❌ load-fixtures.sh not found{Colors.ENDC}")
-
-    def health_check(self):
-        """Check API health"""
-        print(f"\n{Colors.BLUE}🏥 Checking PowerBank API Health...{Colors.ENDC}")
-        
-        # Check if containers are running
-        status = self.run_command(f"docker-compose -f {self.compose_file} ps --services --filter status=running", show_output=False)
-        if status:
-            running_services = status.split('\n') if status else []
-            print(f"{Colors.GREEN}Running Services: {len(running_services)}{Colors.ENDC}")
-            for service in running_services:
-                print(f"  ✅ {service}")
-        
-        # Check API endpoint (try both with and without trailing slash)

-            "http://localhost:8010/api/app/health",

-        ]
-        
-        health_passed = False

-                break
-        
-        if not health_passed:
-            print(f"{Colors.RED}❌ API Health Check: FAILED{Colors.ENDC}")
```
